run *regexp.Regexp\\n59: \\tif runStrings != nil {\\n60: \\t\\trun = regexp.MustCompile(strings.Join(runStrings, \\\"|\\\"))\\n61: \\t}\\n62: \\n63: \\tfor _, f := range files {\\n64: \\t\\tif errs := promqltest.LazyLoaderOpts, run *regexp.Regexp, diffFlag, debug, ignoreUnknownFields bool, ts *junitxml.TestSuite) []error {\\n87: \\tb, err := os.ReadFile(filename)\\n88: \\tif err != nil {\\n89: \\t\\tts.Abort(err)\\n90: \\t\\treturn []error{err}\\n91: \\t}\\n92: \\n93: \\tvar unitTestInp unitTestFile\\n94: \\tif err := yaml.UnmarshalStrict(b, &unitTestInp); err != nil {\\n95: \\t\\treturn []error{err}\\n101: \\t}\\n102: \\n103: \\tif unitTestInp.EvaluationInterval == 0 {\\n104: \\t\\tunitTestInp.EvaluationInterval = model.Duration(1 * time.Minute)\\n105: \\t}\\n106: \\n107: \\tevalInterval := time.Duration(unitTestInp.EvaluationInterval)\\n108: \\tts.Settime(time.Now().Format(\\\"2006-01-02T15:04:05\\\"))\\n109: \\t// Giving number for groups mentioned in the file for ordering.\\n110: \\t// Lower number group should be evaluated before higher number group.\\n111: \\tgroupOrderMap := make(map[string]int)\\n112: \\tfor i, gn := range \\t\\t\\tts.Abort(err)\\n116: \\t\\t\\treturn []error{err}\\n117: \\t\\t}\\n118: \\t\\tgroupOrderMap[gn] = i\\n119: \\t}\\n120: \\n121: \\t// Testing.\\n122: \\tvar errs []error\\n123: \\tfor i, t := unitTestInp.RuleFiles...)\\n136: \\t\\tif ers != nil {\\n137: \\t\\t\\tfor _, e := range ers {\\n138: \\t\\t\\t\\ttc.Fail(e.Error())\\n139: \\t\\t\\t}\\n140: \\t\\t\\terrs = append(errs, *regexp.Regexp) bool {\\n151: \\tif run == nil {\\n152: \\t\\treturn true\\n153: \\t}\\n154: \\n155: \\treturn run.MatchString(name)\\n156: }\\n157: \\n158: // unitTestFile holds the contents of a single unit test file.\\n159: type unitTestFile struct {\\n160: \\tRuleFiles          []string       `yaml:\\\"rule_files\\\"`\\n161: \\tEvaluationInterval model.Duration `yaml:\\\"evaluation_interval,omitempty\\\"`\\n162: \\tGroupEvalOrder     []string       `yaml:\\\"group_eval_order\\\"`\\n163: \\tTests              []testGroup    `yaml:\\\"tests\\\"`\\n164: \\tFuzzyCompare       bool           `yaml:\\\"fuzzy_compare,omitempty\\\"`\\n165: }\\n166: \\n167: // resolveAndGlobFilepaths joins all relative paths in a configuration\\n168: // with a given base directory and replaces all globs with matching files.\\n169: func resolveAndGlobFilepaths(baseDir string, utf *unitTestFile) error {\\n170: \\tfor i, rf := range utf.RuleFiles {\\n171: \\t\\tif rf != \\\"\\\" && !filepath.IsAbs(rf) {\\n172: \\t\\t\\tutf.RuleFiles[i] = filepath.Join(baseDir, rf)\\n173: \\t\\t}\\n174: \\t}\\n175: \\n176: \\tvar globbedFiles []string\\n177: \\tfor _, rf := range \\tio.WriteString(w, result)\\n804: }\\n805: \\n806: func (h *Handler) runtimeInfo() (api_v1.RuntimeInfo, error) {\\n807: \\tstatus := api_v1.RuntimeInfo{\\n808: \\t\\tStartTime:      h.birth,\\n809: \tt.Cleanup(func() {\n121: \t\tparser.EnableExperimentalFunctions = false\n122: \t\tparser.ExperimentalDurationExpr = false\n123: \t})\n124: \tparser.EnableExperimentalFunctions = true\n125: engine promql.QueryEngine, newStorage func(testutil.T) storage.Storage) {\n146: \trequire.NoError(t, runTest(t, input, engine, newStorage, false))\n147: }\n148: \n149: // testTest allows tests to be This is a special mode for testing test code execution itself.\n151: func testTest(t testutil.T, input string, engine promql.QueryEngine) error {\n152: \treturn runTest(t, input, engine, newTestStorage, true)\n153: }\n154: \n155: func runTest(t testutil.T, input string, engine promql.QueryEngine, newStorage func(testutil.T) storage.Storage, testingMode bool) error {\n156: \ttest, err := newTest(t, input, testingMode, newStorage)\n157: \n158: \t// Why do this before checking err? newTest() can create the test storage and then return an error,\n159: \t// and we want to make sure to clean that up to avoid leaking goroutines.\n160: \tdefer func() {\n161: \t\tif test == nil {\n162: \t\t\treturn\n163: \t\t}\n164: \t\tif test.storage != nil {\n165: \t\t\ttest.storage.Close()\n166: \t\t}\n167: \t\tif test.cancelCtx != nil {\n168: \t\t\ttest.cancelCtx()\n169: \t\t}\n170: \t}()\n171: \n172: \tif err != nil {\n173: \t\treturn err\n174: \t}\n175: \n176: \tfor _, cmd := range test.cmds {\n177: \t\tif err := test.exec(cmd, engine); err != nil {\n178: \t\t\t// TODO(fabxc): aggregate command errors, yield diffs for result\n179: \t\t\t// comparison errors.\n180: \t\t\treturn err\n181: \t\t}\n182: \t}\n183: \n184: \treturn nil\n185: }\n186: \n187: // test is a sequence of read and write commands that are run\n188: // against a test storage.\n189: type test struct {\n190: \ttestutil.T\n191: \t// testingMode distinguishes between normal execution and test-execution mode.\n192: \ttestingMode bool\n193: \n194: \tcmds []testCommand\n195: \n196: \topen    func(testutil.T) storage.Storage\n197: \tstorage storage.Storage\n198: \n199: \tcontext   context.Context\n200: \tcancelCtx context.CancelFunc\n201: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4@ba16cf5a7437eb3b6b71b74557b7a67e1975ad0f/-/blob/promql/promqltest/test.go%3FL1-200"}}}

error was encountered in the storage layer\\n97: \\t// during query handling.\\n98: \\tErrStorage struct{ Err error }\\n99: )\\n100: \\n101: func (e ErrQueryTimeout) Error() string {\\n102: \\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"query timed out in %s\\\", string(e))\\n103: }\\n104: \\n105: func (e ErrQueryCanceled) Error() string {\\n106: \\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"query was canceled in %s\\\", string(e))\\n107: }\\n108: \\n109: func (e ErrTooManySamples) Error() string {\\n110: \\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"query processing would load too many samples into memory in %s\\\", string(e))\\n111: }\\n112: \\n113: func (e ErrStorage) Error() string {\\n114: \\treturn e.Err.Error()\\n115: }\\n116: \\n117: // QueryEngine defines the interface for the *promql.Engine, so it can be replaced, wrapped or mocked.\\n118: type QueryEngine interface {\\n119: \\tNewInstantQuery(ctx context.Context, q storage.Queryable, opts QueryOpts, qs string, ts time.Time) (Query, error)\\n120: \\tNewRangeQuery(ctx context.Context, q storage.Queryable, opts QueryOpts, qs string, start, end time.Time, interval time.Duration) (Query, error)\\n121: }\\n122: \\n123: var _ QueryLogger = (*logging.JSONFileLogger)(nil)\\n124: \\n125: // QueryLogger is an interface that can be used to log all the queries logged\\n126: // by the engine.\\n127: type QueryLogger interface {\\n128: \\tslog.Handler\\n129: \\tio.Closer\\n130: }\\n131: \\n132: // A Query is derived from a raw query string and can be run against an engine\\n133: // it is associated with.\\n134: type Query interface {\\n135: \\t// Exec processes the query. Can only be called once.\\n136: \\tExec(ctx context.Context) *Result\\n137: \\t// Close recovers memory used by the query result.\\n138: \\tClose()\\n139: \\t// Statement returns the parsed statement of the query.\\n140: \\tStatement() parser.Statement\\n141: \\t// Stats returns statistics about the lifetime of the query.\\n142: \\tStats() *stats.Statistics\\n143: \\t// Cancel signals that a running query execution should be aborted.\\n144: \\tCancel()\\n145: \\t// String returns the original query string.\\n146: \\tString() string\\n147: }\\n148: \\n149: type PrometheusQueryOpts struct {\\n150: \\t// Enables recording per-step statistics if the engine has it enabled as well. Disabled by default.\\n151: \\tenablePerStepStats bool\\n152: \\t// Lookback delta duration for this query.\\n153: \\tlookbackDelta time.Duration\\n154: }\\n155: \\n156: var _ QueryOpts = &PrometheusQueryOpts{}\\n157: \\n158: func NewPrometheusQueryOpts(enablePerStepStats bool, lookbackDelta time.Duration) QueryOpts {\\n159: \\treturn &PrometheusQueryOpts{\\n160: \\t\\tenablePerStepStats: enablePerStepStats,\\n161: \\t\\tlookbackDelta:      lookbackDelta,\\n162: \\t}\\n163: }\\n164: \\n165: func (p *PrometheusQueryOpts) EnablePerStepStats() bool {\\n166: \\treturn p.enablePerStepStats\\n167: }\\n168: \\n169: func (p *PrometheusQueryOpts) LookbackDelta() time.Duration {\\n170: \\treturn p.lookbackDelta\\n171: }\\n172: \\n173: type QueryOpts interface {\\n174: \\t// Enables recording per-step statistics if the engine has it enabled as well. Disabled by default.\\n175: \\tEnablePerStepStats() bool\\n176: \\t// Lookback delta duration for this query.\\n177: \\tLookbackDelta() time.Duration\\n178: }\\n179: \\n180: // query implements the Query interface.\\n181: type query struct {\\n182: \\t// Underlying data provider.\\n183: \\tqueryable storage.Queryable\\n184: \\t// The original query string.\\n185: \\tq string\\n186: \\t// Statement of the parsed query.\\n187: \\tstmt parser.Statement\\n188: \\t// Timer stats for the query execution.\\n189: \\tstats *stats.QueryTimers\\n190: \\t// Sample stats for the query execution.\\n191: \\tsampleStats *stats.QuerySamples\\n192: \\t// Result matrix for reuse.\\n193: \\tmatrix Matrix\\n194: \\t// Cancellation function for the query.\\n195: \\tcancel func()\\n196: \\n197: \\t// The engine against which the query is executed.\\n198: \\tng *Engine\\n199: }\\n200: \\n201: type QueryOrigin struct{}\\n202: \\n203: // Statement implements the Query interface.\\n204: // Calling this after Exec may result in panic,\\n205: // see https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/issues/8949.\\n206: func (q *query) Statement() parser.Statement {\\n207: \\treturn q.stmt\\n208: }\\n209: \\n210: // String implements the Query interface.\\n211: func (q *query) String() string {\\n212: \\treturn q.q\\n213: }\\n214: \\n215: // Stats implements the Query interface.\\n216: func (q *query) Stats() *stats.Statistics {\\n217: \\treturn &stats.Statistics{\\n218: \\t\\tTimers:  q.stats,\\n219: \\t\\tSamples: q.sampleStats,\\n220: \\t}\\n221: }\\n222: \\n223: // Cancel implements the Query interface.\\n224: func (q *query) Cancel() {\\n225: \\tif q.cancel != nil {\\n226: \\t\\tq.cancel()\\n227: \\t}\\n228: }\\n229: \\n230: // Close implements the Query interface.\\n231: func (q *query) Close() {\\n232: \\tfor _, s := range q.matrix {\\n233: \\t\\tputFPointSlice(s.Floats)\\n234: \\t\\tputHPointSlice(s.Histograms)\\n235: \\t}\\n236: }\\n237: \\n238: // Exec implements the Query interface.\\n239: func (q *query) Exec(ctx context.Context) *Result {\\n240: \\tif span := trace.SpanFromContext(ctx); span != nil {\\n241: \\t\\tspan.SetAttributes(attribute.String(queryTag, q.stmt.String()))\\n242: \\t}\\n243: \\n244: \\t// Exec query.\\n245: \\tres, warnings, err := q.ng.exec(ctx, q)\\n246: \\treturn &Result{Err: err, Value: res, Warnings: warnings}\\n247: }\\n248: \\n249: // contextDone returns an error if the context was canceled or timed out.\\n250: func contextDone(ctx context.Context, env string) error {\\n251: \\tif err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {\\n252: \\t\\treturn contextErr(err, env)\\n253: \\t}\\n254: \\treturn nil\\n255: }\\n256: \\n257: func contextErr(err error, env string) error {\\n258: \\tswitch {\\n259: \\tcase errors.Is(err, context.Canceled):\\n260: \\t\\treturn ErrQueryCanceled(env)\\n261: \\tcase errors.Is(err, context.DeadlineExceeded):\\n262: \\t\\treturn ErrQueryTimeout(env)\\n263: \\tdefault:\\n264: \\t\\treturn err\\n265: \\t}\\n266: }\\n267: \\n268: // QueryTracker provides access to two features:\\n269: //\\n270: // 1) Tracking of active query. If PromQL engine crashes while executing any query, such query should be present\\n271: // in the tracker on restart, hence logged. After the logging on restart, the tracker gets emptied.\\n272: //\\n273: // 2) Enforcement of the maximum number of concurrent queries.\\n274: type QueryTracker interface {\\n275: \\tio.Closer\\n276: \\n277: \\t// GetMaxConcurrent returns maximum number of concurrent queries that are allowed by this tracker.\\n278: \\tGetMaxConcurrent() int\\n279: \\n280: \\t// Insert inserts query into query tracker. This call must block if maximum number of queries is already running.\\n281: \\t// If Insert doesn't return error then returned integer value should be used in subsequent Delete call.\\n282: \\t// Insert should return error if context is finished before query can proceed, and integer value returned in this case should be ignored by caller.\\n283: \\tInsert(ctx context.Context, query string) (int, error)\\n284: \\n285: \\t// Delete removes query from activity tracker. InsertIndex is value returned by Insert call.\\n286: \\tDelete(insertIndex int)\\n287: }\\n288: \\n289: // EngineOpts contains configuration options used when creating a new Engine.\\n290: type EngineOpts struct {\\n291: \\tLogger             *slog.Logger\\n292: \\tReg                prometheus.Registerer\\n293: \\tMaxSamples         int\\n294: \\tTimeout            time.Duration\\n295: \\tActiveQueryTracker QueryTracker\\n296: \\t// LookbackDelta determines the time since the last sample after which a time\\n297: \\t// series is considered stale.\\n298: \\tLookbackDelta time.Duration\\n299: \\n300: \\t// NoStepSubqueryIntervalFn is the default evaluation interval of\\n301: \\t// a subquery in milliseconds if no step in range vector was specified `[30m:<step>]`.\\n302: \\tNoStepSubqueryIntervalFn func(rangeMillis int64) int64\\n303: \\n304: \\t// EnableAtModifier if true enables @ modifier. Disabled otherwise. This\\n305: \\t// is supposed to be enabled for regular PromQL (as of Prometheus v2.33)\\n306: \\t// but the option to disable it is still provided here for those using\\n307: \\t// the Engine outside of Prometheus.\\n308: \\tEnableAtModifier bool\\n309: \\n310: \\t// EnableNegativeOffset if true enables negative (-) offset\\n311: \\t// values. Disabled otherwise. This is supposed to be enabled for\\n312: \\t// regular PromQL (as of Prometheus v2.33) but the option to disable it\\n313: \\t// is still provided here for those using the Engine outside of\\n314: \\t// Prometheus.\\n315: \\tEnableNegativeOffset bool\\n316: \\n317: \\t// EnablePerStepStats if true allows for per-step stats to be computed on request. Disabled otherwise.\\n318: \\tEnablePerStepStats bool\\n319: \\n320: \\t// EnableDelayedNameRemoval delays the removal of the __name__ label to the last step of the query evaluation.\\n321: \\t// This is useful in certain scenarios where the __name__ label must be preserved or where applying a\\n322: \\t// regex-matcher to the __name__ label may otherwise lead to duplicate labelset errors.\\n323: \\tEnableDelayedNameRemoval bool\\n324: \\t// EnableTypeAndUnitLabels will allow PromQL Engine to make decisions based on the type and unit labels.\\n325: \\tEnableTypeAndUnitLabels bool\\n326: }\\n327: \\n328: // Engine handles the lifetime of queries from beginning to end.\\n329: // It is connected to a querier.\\n330: type Engine struct {\\n331: \\tlogger derived from a raw query string and can be run against an engine\\n133: // it is associated with.\\n134: type Query interface {\\n135: \\t// Exec processes the query. Disabled by default.\\n151: \\tenablePerStepStats bool\\n152: \\t// Lookback delta duration for this query.\\n153: \\tlookbackDelta time.Duration\\n154: }\\n155: \\n156: var _ QueryOpts = &PrometheusQueryOpts{}\\n157: \\n158: func NewPrometheusQueryOpts(enablePerStepStats bool, lookbackDelta time.Duration) QueryOpts {\\n159: \\treturn &PrometheusQueryOpts{\\n160: \\t\\tenablePerStepStats: enablePerStepStats,\\n161: \\t\\tlookbackDelta:      lookbackDelta,\\n162: \\t}\\n163: }\\n164: \\n165: func (p *PrometheusQueryOpts) EnablePerStepStats() bool {\\n166: \\treturn p.enablePerStepStats\\n167: }\\n168: \\n169: func (p *PrometheusQueryOpts) LookbackDelta() time.Duration {\\n170: \\treturn p.lookbackDelta\\n171: }\\n172: \\n173: type QueryOpts interface {\\n174: \\t// Enables recording per-step statistics if the engine has it enabled as well. Disabled by default.\\n175: \\tEnablePerStepStats() bool\\n176: \\t// Lookback delta duration for this query.\\n177: \\tLookbackDelta() time.Duration\\n178: }\\n179: \\n180: // query implements the Query interface.\\n181: type query struct {\\n182: \\t// Underlying data provider.\\n183: \\tqueryable storage.Queryable\\n184: \\t// The original query string.\\n185: \\tq string\\n186: \\t// Statement of the parsed query.\\n187: \\tstmt parser.Statement\\n188: \\t// Timer stats for the query execution.\\n189: \\tstats *stats.QueryTimers\\n190: \\t// Sample stats for the query execution.\\n191: \\tsampleStats *stats.QuerySamples\\n192: \\t// Result matrix for reuse.\\n193: \\tmatrix Matrix\\n194: \\t// Cancellation function for the query.\\n195: \\tcancel func()\\n196: \\n197: \\t// The engine against which the query is executed.\\n198: \\tng *Engine\\n199: }\\n200: \\n201: type QueryOrigin struct{}\\n202: \\n203: // Statement implements the Query interface.\\n204: // Calling this after Exec may result in panic,\\n205: // see https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/issues/8949.\\n206: func (q *query) Statement() parser.Statement {\\n207: \\treturn q.stmt\\n208: }\\n209: \\n210: // String implements the Query interface.\\n211: func (q *query) String() string {\\n212: \\treturn q.q\\n213: }\\n214: \\n215: // Stats implements the Query interface.\\n216: func (q Query interface.\\n239: func (q *query) Exec(ctx context.Context) *Result {\\n240: \\tif span := trace.SpanFromContext(ctx); span != nil {\\n241: \\t\\tspan.SetAttributes(attribute.String(queryTag, \\n249: // contextDone returns an error if the context was canceled or timed out.\\n250: func contextDone(ctx context.Context, env string) error {\\n251: \\tif err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {\\n252: \\t\\treturn contextErr(err, env)\\n253: \\t}\\n254: \\treturn nil\\n255: }\\n256: \\n257: func contextErr(err error, env string) error {\\n258: \\tswitch {\\n259: \\tcase errors.Is(err, context.Canceled):\\n260: \\t\\treturn ErrQueryCanceled(env)\\n261: \\tcase errors.Is(err, context.DeadlineExceeded):\\n262: \\t\\treturn ErrQueryTimeout(env)\\n263: \\tdefault:\\n264: \\t\\treturn err\\n265: \\t}\\n266: }\\n267: \\n268: // QueryTracker provides access to two features:\\n269: //\\n270: // 1) Tracking of active query. If PromQL engine crashes while executing any query, such query should be present\\n271: // in the tracker on restart, hence logged. After the logging on restart, the tracker gets emptied.\\n272: //\\n273: // 2) Enforcement of the maximum number of concurrent queries.\\n274: type QueryTracker interface {\\n275: \\tio.Closer\\n276: \\n277: \\t// GetMaxConcurrent returns maximum number of concurrent queries that are allowed by this tracker.\\n278: \\tGetMaxConcurrent() int\\n279: \\n280: \\t// Insert inserts query into query tracker. This call must block if maximum number of queries is already running.\\n281: \\t// If Insert doesn't return error then returned integer value should be used in subsequent Delete call.\\n282: \\t// Insert should return error if context is finished before query can proceed, and integer value returned in this case should be ignored by caller.\\n283: \\tInsert(ctx context.Context, query string) (int, error)\\n284: \\n285: \\t// Delete removes query from activity tracker. InsertIndex is value returned by Insert call.\\n286: \\tDelete(insertIndex int)\\n287: }\\n288: \\n289: // EngineOpts contains configuration options used when creating a new Engine.\\n290: type EngineOpts struct {\\n291: \\tLogger             *slog.Logger\\n292: \\tReg                prometheus.Registerer\\n293: \\tMaxSamples         int\\n294: \\tTimeout            time.Duration\\n295: \\tActiveQueryTracker QueryTracker\\n296: \\t// LookbackDelta determines the time since the last sample after which a time\\n297: \\t// series is considered stale.\\n298: \\tLookbackDelta time.Duration\\n299: \\n300: \\t// NoStepSubqueryIntervalFn is the default evaluation interval of\\n301: \\t// a subquery in milliseconds if no step in range vector was specified `[30m:<step>]`.\\n302: \\tNoStepSubqueryIntervalFn func(rangeMillis int64) int64\\n303: \\n304: \\t// EnableAtModifier if true enables @ modifier. Disabled otherwise. This\\n305: \\t// is supposed to be enabled for regular PromQL (as of Prometheus v2.33)\\n306: \\t// but the option to disable it is still provided here for those using\\n307: \\t// the Engine outside of Prometheus.\\n308: \\tEnableAtModifier bool\\n309: \\n310: \\t// EnableNegativeOffset if true enables negative (-) offset\\n311: \\t// values. Disabled otherwise. This is supposed to be enabled for\\n312: \\t// regular PromQL (as of Prometheus v2.33) but the option to disable it\\n313: \\t// is still provided here for those using the Engine outside of\\n314: \\t// Prometheus.\\n315: \\tEnableNegativeOffset bool\\n316: \\n317: \\t// EnablePerStepStats if true allows for per-step stats to be computed on request. Disabled otherwise.\\n318: \\tEnablePerStepStats bool\\n319: \\n320: \\t// EnableDelayedNameRemoval delays the removal of the __name__ label to the last step of the query evaluation.\\n321: \\t// This is useful in certain scenarios where the __name__ label must be preserved or where applying a\\n322: \\t// regex-matcher to the __name__ label may otherwise lead to duplicate labelset errors.\\n323: \\tEnableDelayedNameRemoval bool\\n324: \\t// EnableTypeAndUnitLabels will allow PromQL Engine to make decisions based on the type and unit labels.\\n325: \\tEnableTypeAndUnitLabels bool\\n326: }\\n327: \\n328: // Engine handles the lifetime of queries from beginning to end.\\n329: // It is connected to a querier.\\n330: type Engine struct {\\n331: \\tlogger If PromQL engine crashes while executing any query, such query should be present\n271: // in the tracker on restart, hence logged. After the logging on restart, the tracker gets emptied.\n272: //\n273: // 2) Enforcement of the maximum number of concurrent queries.\n274: type QueryTracker interface {\n275: \tio.Closer\n276: \n277: \t// GetMaxConcurrent returns maximum number of concurrent queries that are allowed by this tracker.\n278: \tGetMaxConcurrent() int\n279: \n280: \t// Insert inserts query into query tracker. This call must block if maximum number of queries is already running.\n281: \t// If Insert doesn't return error then returned integer value should be used in subsequent Delete call.\n282: \t// Insert should return error if context is finished before query can proceed, and integer value returned in this case should be ignored by caller.\n283: \tInsert(ctx context.Context, query string) (int, error)\n284: \n285: \t// Delete removes query from activity tracker. InsertIndex is value returned by Insert call.\n286: \tDelete(insertIndex int)\n287: }\n288: \n289: // EngineOpts contains configuration options used when creating a new Engine.\n290: type EngineOpts struct {\n291: \tLogger             *slog.Logger\n292: \tReg                prometheus.Registerer\n293: \tMaxSamples         int\n294: \tTimeout            time.Duration\n295: \tActiveQueryTracker QueryTracker\n296: \t// LookbackDelta determines the time since the last sample after which a time\n297: \t// series is considered stale.\n298: \tLookbackDelta time.Duration\n299: \n300: \t// NoStepSubqueryIntervalFn is the default evaluation interval of\n301: \t// a subquery in milliseconds if no step in range vector was specified `[30m:<step>]`.\n302: \tNoStepSubqueryIntervalFn func(rangeMillis int64) int64\n303: \n304: \t// EnableAtModifier if true enables @ modifier. useful in certain scenarios where the __name__ label must be preserved or where applying a\n322: \t// regex-matcher to the __name__ label may otherwise lead to duplicate labelset errors.\n323: \\n249: // contextDone returns an error if the context was canceled or timed out.\\n250: func contextDone(ctx context.Context, env string) error {\\n251: \\tif err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {\\n252: err\\n265: \\t}\\n266: }\\n267: \\n268: // QueryTracker provides access to two features:\\n269: //\\n270: // 1) Tracking of active query. If PromQL engine crashes while executing any query, such query should be present\\n271: // in the tracker on restart, hence logged. After the logging on restart, the tracker gets emptied.\\n272: //\\n273: // 2) Enforcement of the maximum number of concurrent queries.\\n274: type QueryTracker interface {\\n275: \\tio.Closer\\n276: \\n277: \\t// GetMaxConcurrent returns maximum number of concurrent queries that are allowed by this tracker.\\n278: \\tGetMaxConcurrent() int\\n279: \\n280: \\t// Insert inserts query into query tracker. This call must block if maximum number of queries is already running.\\n281: \\t// If Insert doesn't return error then returned integer value should be used in subsequent Delete call.\\n282: \\t// Insert should return error if context is finished before query can proceed, and integer value returned in this case should be ignored by caller.\\n283: \\tInsert(ctx context.Context, query string) (int, error)\\n284: \\n285: \\t// Delete removes query from activity tracker. InsertIndex is value returned by Insert call.\\n286: \\tDelete(insertIndex int)\\n287: }\\n288: \\n289: // EngineOpts contains configuration options used when creating a new Engine.\\n290: type EngineOpts struct {\\n291: \\tLogger             *slog.Logger\\n292: \\tReg                prometheus.Registerer\\n293: \\tMaxSamples         int\\n294: \\tTimeout            time.Duration\\n295: \\tActiveQueryTracker QueryTracker\\n296: \\t// LookbackDelta determines the time since the last sample after which a time\\n297: \\t// series is considered stale.\\n298: \\tLookbackDelta time.Duration\\n299: \\n300: \\t// NoStepSubqueryIntervalFn is the default evaluation interval of\\n301: \\t// a subquery in milliseconds if no step in range vector was specified `[30m:<step>]`.\\n302: \\tNoStepSubqueryIntervalFn func(rangeMillis int64) int64\\n303: \\n304: \\t// EnableAtModifier if true enables @ modifier. Disabled otherwise. This\\n305: \\t// is supposed to be enabled for regular PromQL (as of Prometheus v2.33)\\n306: \\t// but the option to disable it is still provided here for those using\\n307: \\t// the Engine outside of Prometheus.\\n308: \\tEnableAtModifier bool\\n309: \\n310: \\t// EnableNegativeOffset if true enables negative (-) offset\\n311: \\t// values. Disabled otherwise. This is supposed to be enabled for\\n312: \\t// regular PromQL (as of Prometheus v2.33) but the option to disable it\\n313: \\t// is still provided here for those using the Engine outside of\\n314: \\t// Prometheus.\\n315: \\tEnableNegativeOffset bool\\n316: \\n317: \\t// EnablePerStepStats if true allows for per-step stats to be computed on request. Disabled otherwise.\\n318: \\tEnablePerStepStats bool\\n319: \\n320: \\t// EnableDelayedNameRemoval delays the removal of the __name__ label to the last step of the query evaluation.\\n321: \\t// This is useful in certain scenarios where the __name__ label must be preserved or where applying a\\n322: \\t// regex-matcher to the __name__ label may otherwise lead to duplicate labelset errors.\\n323: \\tEnableDelayedNameRemoval bool\\n324: \\t// EnableTypeAndUnitLabels will allow PromQL Engine to make decisions based on the type and unit labels.\\n325: \\tEnableTypeAndUnitLabels bool\\n326: }\\n327: \\n328: // Engine handles the lifetime of queries from beginning to end.\\n329: // It is connected to a querier.\\n330: type Engine struct {\\n331: \\tlogger   bool\\n1729: \\t\\t\\twarnings       annotations.Annotations\\n1730: \\t\\t)\\n1731: \\t\\tfor i := range e.Args {\\n1732: \\t\\t\\ta := e.Args[i]\\n1733: \\t\\t\\tif _, ok := \\t\\tev.currentSamples += histograms[n].size()\\n2534: \\t\\tif ev.currentSamples > ev.maxSamples {\\n2535: \\t\\t\\tev.error(ErrTooManySamples(env))\\n2536: \\t\\t}\\n2537: \\n2538: \\tcase chunkenc.ValFloat:\\n2539: \\t\\tt, f := it.At()\\n2540: \\t\\tif t == maxt && !value.IsStaleNaN(f) {\\n2541: \\t\\t\\tev.currentSamples++\\n2542: \\t\\t\\tif ev.currentSamples > ev.maxSamples {\\n2543: \\t\\t\\t\\tev.error(ErrTooManySamples(env))\\n2544: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2545: \\t\\t\\tif floats == nil {\\n2546: \\t\\t\\t\\tfloats = getFPointSlice(16)\\n2547: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2548: \\t\\t\\tfloats (*evaluator) VectorAnd(lhs, rhs Vector, matching *parser.VectorMatching, lhsh, rhsh []EvalSeriesHelper, enh *EvalNodeHelper) Vector {\\n2556: \\tif matching.Card != parser.CardManyToMany {\\n2557: \\t\\tpanic(\\\"set operations must only use many-to-many matching\\\")\\n2558: \\t}\\n2559: \\tif len(lhs) == 0 || len(rhs) == 0 {\\n2560: \\t\\treturn nil // Short-circuit: AND with nothing is nothing.\\n2561: \\t}\\n2562: \\n2563: \\t// The set of signatures for the right-hand side Vector.\\n2564: \\trightSigs := map[string]struct{}{}\\n2565: \\t// Add all rhs samples to a map so we can easily find matches later.\\n2566: \\tfor _, sh := range rhsh {\\n2567: \\t\\trightSigs[sh.signature] = struct{}{}\\n2568: \\t}\\n2569: \\n2570: \\tfor i, ls := range lhs {\\n2571: \\t\\t// If there's \\t}\\n2576: \\treturn enh.Out\\n2577: }\\n2578: \\n2579: func (*evaluator) VectorOr(lhs, rhs Vector, matching *parser.VectorMatching, lhsh, rhsh []EvalSeriesHelper, enh *EvalNodeHelper) Vector {\\n2580: \\tswitch {\\n2581: \\tcase matching.Card != parser.CardManyToMany:\\n2582: \\t\\tpanic(\\\"set operations must only use many-to-many matching\\\")\\n2583: \\tcase len(lhs) == 0: // Short-circuit.\\n2584: \\t\\tenh.Out = append(enh.Out, rhs...)\\n2585: \\t\\treturn enh.Out\\n2586: \\tcase len(rhs) == 0:\\n2587: \\t\\tenh.Out = append(enh.Out, lhs...)\\n2588: \\t\\treturn enh.Out\\n2604: }\\n2605: \\n2606: func (*evaluator) VectorUnless(lhs, rhs Vector, matching *parser.VectorMatching, lhsh, rhsh []EvalSeriesHelper, enh *EvalNodeHelper) Vector {\\n2607: \\tif matching.Card != parser.CardManyToMany {\\n2608: \\t\\tpanic(\\\"set operations must only use many-to-many matching\\\")\\n2609: \\t}\\n2610: \\t// Short-circuit: empty rhs means we will return everything in lhs;\\n2611: \\t// empty lhs means we will return empty - don't need to build a map.\\n2612: \\tif len(lhs) == 0 || len(rhs) == 0 {\\n2613: \\t\\tenh.Out = append(enh.Out, \\t}\\n2621: \\n2622: \\tfor i, ls := range lhs {\\n2623: \\t\\tif _, ok := rightSigs[lhsh[i].signature]; !ok {\\n2624: \\t\\t\\tenh.Out = append(enh.Out, ls)\\n2625: \\t\\t}\\n2626: \\t}\\n2627: \\treturn enh.Out\\n2628: }\\n2629: \\n2630: // VectorBinop evaluates a binary operation between two Vectors, excluding set operators.\\n2631: func (ev *evaluator) VectorBinop(op parser.ItemType, lhs, parser.CardManyToMany {\\n2633: \\t\\tpanic(\\\"many-to-many only allowed for set operators\\\")\\n2634: \\t}\\n2635: \\tif len(lhs) == 0 || len(rhs) == 0 {\\n2636: \\t\\treturn nil, nil // Short-circuit: nothing is going to match.\\n2637: \\t}\\n2638: \\n2639: \\t// The control flow below handles one-to-one or many-to-one matching.\\n2640: \\t// For one-to-many, swap sidedness and For floats, we improve the accuracy with the help\\n3104: \\t\\t\\t// of Kahan summation. For a while, we assumed that\\n3105: \\t\\t\\t// incremental mean calculation combined with Kahan\\n3106: \\t\\t\\t// summation (see\\n3107: \\t\\t\\t// https://stackoverflow.com/questions/61665473/is-it-beneficial-for-precision-to-calculate-the-incremental-mean-average\\n3108: \\t\\t\\t// for inspiration) is generally the preferred solution.\\n3109: \\t\\t\\t// However, it then turned out that direct mean\\n3110: \\t\\t\\t// calculation (still in combination with Kahan\\n3111: \\t\\t\\t// summation) is often more accurate. See discussion in\\n3112: \\t\\t\\t// https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/issues/16714\\n3113: \\t\\t\\t// . The problem with the direct mean calculation is\\n3114: \\t\\t\\t// that it can overflow float64 for inputs on which the\\n3115: \\t\\t\\t// incremental mean calculation works just fine. Our\\n3116: \\t\\t\\t// current approach is therefore to use direct mean\\n3117: \\t\\t\\t// calculation as long as we do not overflow (or\\n3118: \\t\\t\\t// underflow) the running sum. Once the latter would\\n3119: \\t\\t\\t// happen, we switch to incremental mean calculation.\\n3120: \\t\\t\\t// This seems to work reasonably well, but note that a\\n3121: \\t\\t\\t// deeper understanding would be needed to find out if\\n3122: \\t\\t\\t// maybe an earlier switch to incremental mean\\n3123: \\t\\t\\t// calculation would be better in terms of accuracy.\\n3124: \\t\\t\\t// Also, we could apply a number of additional means to\\n3125: \\t\\t\\t// improve the accuracy, like processing the values in a\\n3126: \\t\\t\\t// particular order. \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\\\"bottomk\\\", e.PosRange))\\n3389: \\t\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n3390: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\theap.Push(&group.heap, &s)\\n3391: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n3392: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3393: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n3394: \\t\\t}\\n3395: \\n3396: \\t\\tswitch op {\\n3397: \\t\\tcase parser.TOPK:\\n3398: \\t\\t\\t// We build a heap of up to k elements, with the smallest element at heap[0].\\n3399: \\t\\t\\tswitch {\\n3400: \\t\\t\\tcase s.H != nil:\\n3401: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Ignore histogram sample and add info annotation.\\n3402: \\t\\t\\t\\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\\\"topk\\\", e.PosRange))\\n3403: \\t\\t\\tcase int64(len(group.heap)) < k:\\n3404: \\t\\t\\t\\theap.Push(&group.heap, &s)\\n3405: \\t\\t\\tcase group.heap[0].F < s.F || (math.IsNaN(group.heap[0].F) && !math.IsNaN(s.F)):\\n3406: \\t\\t\\t\\t// This new element is bigger than the previous smallest element - overwrite that.\\n3407: \\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.heap[0] = s\\n3408: \\t\\t\\t\\tif k > 1 {\\n3409: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\theap.Fix(&group.heap, 0) // Maintain the heap invariant.\\n3410: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n3411: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3412: \\n3413: \\t\\tcase parser.BOTTOMK:\\n3414: \\t\\t\\t// We build a heap of up to k elements, with the biggest element at heap[0].\\n3415: \\t\\t\\tswitch {\\n3416: \\t\\t\\tcase s.H != nil:\\n3417: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Ignore histogram sample and add info annotation.\\n3418: \\t\\t\\t\\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\\\"bottomk\\\", e.PosRange))\\n3419: \\t\\t\\tcase int64(len(group.heap)) < k:\\n3420: \\t\\t\\t\\theap.Push((*vectorByReverseValueHeap)(&group.heap), &s)\\n3421: \\t\\t\\tcase group.heap[0].F > s.F || (math.IsNaN(group.heap[0].F) && !math.IsNaN(s.F)):\\n3422: \\t\\t\\t\\t// This new element is smaller than the previous biggest element - overwrite that.\\n3423: \\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.heap[0] = s\\n3424: \\t\\t\\t\\tif k > 1 {\\n3425: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\theap.Fix((*vectorByReverseValueHeap)(&group.heap), 0) // Maintain the heap invariant.\\n3426: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n3427: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3428: \\n3429: \\t\\tcase parser.LIMITK:\\n3430: group,\\n3434: \\t\\t\\t// especially useful for large timeseries where the user is exploring labels via e.g.\\n3435: \\t\\t\\t// limitk(10, my_metric)\\n3436: \\t\\t\\tif !group.groupAggrComplete && int64(len(group.heap)) == k {\\n3437: \\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.groupAggrComplete = true\\n3438: \\t\\t\\t\\tgroupsRemaining--\\n3439: \\t\\t\\t\\tif groupsRemaining == 0 {\\n3440: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// Process other values in the series before breaking the loop in case of range query.\\n3441: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif enh.Ts != ev.endTimestamp {\\n3442: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tadvanceRemainingSeries(enh.Ts, si+1)\\n3443: {\\n3450: \\t\\t\\t\\theap.Push(&group.heap, &s)\\n3451: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3452: \\n3453: \\t\\tdefault:\\n3454: \\t\\t\\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\\\"expected aggregation operator but got %q\\\", op))\\n3455: \\t\\t}\\n3456: \\t}\\n3457: \\n3458: \\t// Construct the result from the aggregated groups.\\n3459: \\tnumSteps := int((ev.endTimestamp-ev.startTimestamp)/ev.interval) + 1\\n3460: \\tvar mat Matrix\\n3461: \\tif ev.endTimestamp == ev.startTimestamp {\\n3462: \\t\\tmat = make(Matrix, 0, len(groups))\\n3463: \\t}\\n3464: \\n3465: \\tadd := func(lbls labels.Labels, f float64, h as many series per group as there are values in the input.\\n3519: func (*evaluator) aggregationCountValues(e *parser.AggregateExpr, grouping []string, valueLabel string, vec Vector, enh expression.\\n3703: func unwrapParenExpr(e *parser.Expr) {\\n3704: \\tfor {\\n3705: \\t\\tp, ok := (*e).(*parser.ParenExpr)\\n3706: \\t\\tif !ok {\\n3707: \\t\\t\\tbreak\\n3708: \\t\\t}\\n3709: p.Expr\\n3716: \\t}\\n3717: \\treturn e\\n3718: }\\n3719: \\n3720: // PreprocessExpr wraps all possible step invariant parts of the given expression with\\n3721: // StepInvariantExpr. It also resolves the preprocessors, evaluates duration expressions\\n3722: // into their numeric values and removes superfluous parenthesis on parameters to functions and aggregations.\\n3723: func PreprocessExpr(expr parser.Expr, start, end time.Time, step time.Duration) (parser.Expr, error) {\\n3724: \\tdetectHistogramStatsDecoding(expr)\\n3725: \\n3726: \\tif err := parser.Walk(&durationVisitor{step: step}, expr, nil); err != nil {\\n3727: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n3728: \\t}\\n3729: \\n3730: \\t_, shouldWrap := preprocessExprHelper(expr, start, end)\\n3731: \\tif shouldWrap {\\n3732: \\t\\treturn newStepInvariantExpr(expr), nil\\n3733: \\t}\\n3734: \\treturn expr, nil\\n3735: }\\n3736: \\n3737: // preprocessExprHelper wraps child nodes of expr with a StepInvariantExpr,\\n3738: // at the highest level within the tree that is step-invariant.\\n3739: // Also resolves start() and end() on selector and subquery nodes.\\n3740: // Also remove superfluous parenthesis on parameters to functions and aggregations.\\n3741: // Return isStepInvariant is true when the whole subexpression is step invariant.\\n3742: // Return shoudlWrap is false for cases like MatrixSelector and StringLiteral that never need to be wrapped.\\n3743: func preprocessExprHelper(expr parser.Expr, start, end time.Time) (isStepInvariant, shouldWrap bool) {\\n3744: \\tswitch n := expr.(type) {\\n3745: \\tcase *parser.VectorSelector:\\n3746: \\t\\tswitch n.StartOrEnd {\\n3747: \\t\\tcase parser.START:\\n3748: \\t\\t\\tn.Timestamp = makeInt64Pointer(timestamp.FromTime(start))\\n3749: \\t\\tcase parser.END:\\n3750: \\t\\t\\tn.Timestamp = makeInt64Pointer(timestamp.FromTime(end))\\n3751: \\t\\t}\\n3752: \\t\\treturn n.Timestamp != nil, n.Timestamp != nil\\n3753: \\n3754: \\tcase *parser.AggregateExpr:\\n3755: \\t\\tunwrapParenExpr(&n.Expr)\\n3756: \\t\\tunwrapParenExpr(&n.Param)\\n3757: \\t\\treturn preprocessExprHelper(n.Expr, start, end)\\n3758: \\n3759: \\tcase *parser.BinaryExpr:\\n3760: \\t\\tisInvariantLHS, shouldWrapLHS := preprocessExprHelper(n.LHS, start, end)\\n3761: \\t\\tisInvariantRHS, shouldWrapRHS := preprocessExprHelper(n.RHS, start, end)\\n3762: \\t\\tif isInvariantLHS && isInvariantRHS {\\n3763: \\t\\t\\treturn true, true\\n3764: \\t\\t}\\n3765: \\n3766: \\t\\tif shouldWrapLHS {\\n3767: \\t\\t\\tn.LHS = newStepInvariantExpr(n.LHS)\\n3768: \\t\\t}\\n3769: \\t\\tif shouldWrapRHS {\\n3770: \\t\\t\\tn.RHS = newStepInvariantExpr(n.RHS)\\n3771: \\t\\t}\\n3772: \\n3773: \\t\\treturn false, false\\n3774: \\n3775: \\tcase *parser.Call:\\n3776: \\t\\t_, ok := AtModifierUnsafeFunctions[n.Func.Name]\\n3777: \\t\\tisStepInvariant := !ok\\n3778: \\t\\tshouldWrap := make([]bool, len(n.Args))\\n3779: \\t\\tfor i := range n.Args {\\n3780: \\t\\t\\tunwrapParenExpr(&n.Args[i])\\n3781: \\t\\t\\tvar argIsStepInvariant bool\\n3782: \\t\\t\\targIsStepInvariant, shouldWrap[i] = preprocessExprHelper(n.Args[i], start, end)\\n3783: \\t\\t\\tisStepInvariant = isStepInvariant && argIsStepInvariant\\n3784: \\t\\t}\\n3785: \\n3786: \\t\\tif isStepInvariant {\\n3787: \\t\\t\\t// The function and all arguments are step invariant.\\n3788: \\t\\t\\treturn true, true\\n3789: \\t\\t}\\n3790: \\n3791: \\t\\tfor i, isi := range shouldWrap {\\n3792: \\t\\t\\tif isi {\\n3793: \\t\\t\\t\\tn.Args[i] = newStepInvariantExpr(n.Args[i])\\n3794: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3795: \\t\\t}\\n3796: \\t\\treturn false, false\\n3797: \\n3798: \\tcase *parser.MatrixSelector:\\n3799: \\t\\t// We don't need to wrap a MatrixSelector because functions over range vectors evaluate those directly.\\n3800: \\t\\tisStepInvariant, _ := preprocessExprHelper(n.VectorSelector, start, end)\\n3801: \\t\\treturn isStepInvariant, false\\n3802: \\n3803: \\tcase *parser.SubqueryExpr:\\n3804: \\t\\t// Since we adjust offset for the @ modifier evaluation,\\n3805: \\t\\t// it gets tricky to adjust it for every subquery step.\\n3806: \\t\\t// Hence we wrap the inside of subquery irrespective of\\n3807: \\t\\t// @ on subquery (given it is also step invariant) so that\\n3808: \\t\\t// it is evaluated only once w.r.t. the start time of subquery.\\n3809: \\t\\tisInvariant, _ := preprocessExprHelper(n.Expr, start, end)\\n3810: \\t\\tif isInvariant {\\n3811: \\t\\t\\tn.Expr = newStepInvariantExpr(n.Expr)\\n3812: \\t\\t}\\n3813: \\t\\tswitch n.StartOrEnd {\\n3814: \\t\\tcase parser.START:\\n3815: \\t\\t\\tn.Timestamp = makeInt64Pointer(timestamp.FromTime(start))\\n3816: \\t\\tcase parser.END:\\n3817: \\t\\t\\tn.Timestamp = makeInt64Pointer(timestamp.FromTime(end))\\n3818: \\t\\t}\\n3819: \\t\\treturn n.Timestamp != nil, n.Timestamp != nil\\n3820: \\n3821: \\tcase setOffsetForAtModifier(evalTime int64, expr parser.Expr) {\\n3842: \\tgetOffset := func(ts *int64, originalOffset time.Duration, path []parser.Node) time.Duration {\\n3843: \\t\\tif ts == nil subqOffset\\n3854: \\t\\treturn originalOffset + offsetDiff\\n3855: \\t}\\n3856: \\n3857: \\tparser.Inspect(expr, func(node parser.Node, path []parser.Node) error {\\n3858: \\t\\tswitch n := spans\\n3876: // and buckets. The function can be treated as an optimization and is not\\n3877: // required for correctness.\\n3878: func detectHistogramStatsDecoding(expr parser.Expr) {\\n3879: \\tparser.Inspect(expr, func(node \\n3934: func (s *HashRatioSampler) AddRatioSample(ratioLimit float64, sample *Sample) bool {\\n3935: \\t// If ratioLimit >= 0: add sample if sampleOffset is lesser than ratioLimit\\n3936: \\t//\\n3937: \\t// 0.0        ratioLimit                1.0\\n3938: \\t//  [---------|--------------------------]\\n3939: \\t//  [#########...........................]\\n3940: \\t//\\n3941: \\t// e.g.:\\n3942: \\t//   sampleOffset==0.3 && ratioLimit==0.4\\n3943: \\t//     0.3 < 0.4 ? --> add sample\\n3944: \\t//\\n3945: \\t// Else if ratioLimit < 0: add sample if rand() return the \\\"complement\\\" of ratioLimit>=0 case\\n3946: \\t// (loosely similar behavior to negative array index in other programming languages)\\n3947: \\t//\\n3948: \\t// 0.0       1+ratioLimit \\n185: \\treturn m\\n186: }\\n187: \\n188: // Run starts processing of the rule manager. It is blocking.\\n189: func (m *Manager) Run() {\\n190: \\tm.logger.Info(\\\"Starting rule manager...\\\")\\n191: \\tm.start()\\n192: \\t<-m.done\\n193: }\\n194: \\n195: func (m *Manager) start() {\\n196: \\tclose(m.block)\\n197: }\\n198: \\n199: // Stop the rule manager's rule evaluation cycles.\\n200: func (m *Manager) Stop() {\\n201: \\tm.mtx.Lock()\\n202: \\tdefer m.mtx.Unlock()\\n203: \\n204: \\tm.logger.Info(\\\"Stopping rule manager...\\\")\\n205: \\n206: \\t// Stop all groups asynchronously, then wait for them to finish.\\n207: \\t// This is faster than stopping and waiting for each group in sequence.\\n208: \\tfor _, eg := range m.groups {\\n209: \\t\\teg.stopAsync()\\n210: \\t}\\n211: \\n212: \\tfor _, eg := range m.groups {\\n213: \\t\\teg.waitStopped()\\n214: \\t}\\n215: \\n216: \\t// Shut down the groups waiting multiple evaluation intervals to write\\n217: \\t// staleness markers.\\n218: \\tclose(m.done)\\n219: \\n220: \\tm.logger.Info(\\\"Rule manager stopped\\\")\\n221: }\\n222: \\n223: // Update the rule manager's state as the config requires. If\\n224: // loading the new rules failed the old rule set is restored.\\n225: // This method will no-op in case the manager is already stopped.\\n226: func (m *Manager) Update(interval time.Duration, files []string, externalLabels labels.Labels, externalURL string, groupEvalIterationFunc GroupEvalIterationFunc) error {\\n227: \\tm.mtx.Lock()\\n228: \\tdefer m.mtx.Unlock()\\n229: \\n230: \\t// We cannot update a stopped manager\\n231: \\tselect {\\n232: \\tcase <-m.done:\\n233: \\t\\treturn nil\\n234: \\tdefault:\\n235: \\t}\\n236: \\n237: \\tgroups, errs := m.LoadGroups(interval, externalLabels, externalURL, groupEvalIterationFunc, false, files...)\\n238: \\n239: \\tif errs != nil {\\n240: \\t\\tfor _, e := range errs {\\n241: \\t\\t\\tm.logger.Error(\\\"loading groups failed\\\", \\\"err\\\", e)\\n242: \\t\\t}\\n243: \\t\\treturn errors.New(\\\"error loading rules, previous rule set restored\\\")\\n244: \\t}\\n245: \\tm.restored = true\\n246: \\n247: \\tvar wg sync.WaitGroup\\n248: \\tfor _, newg := range groups {\\n249: \\t\\t// If there is an old group with the same identifier,\\n250: \\t\\t// check if new group equals with the old group, if yes then skip it.\\n251: \\t\\t// If not equals, stop it and wait for it to finish the current iteration.\\n252: \\t\\t// Then copy it into the new group.\\n253: \\t\\tgn := GroupKey(newg.file, newg.name)\\n254: \\t\\toldg, ok := m.groups[gn]\\n255: \\t\\tdelete(m.groups, gn)\\n256: \\n257: \\t\\tif ok && oldg.Equals(newg) {\\n258: \\t\\t\\tgroups[gn] = oldg\\n259: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n260: \\t\\t}\\n261: \\n262: \\t\\twg.Add(1)\\n263: \\t\\tgo func(newg *Group) {\\n264: \\t\\t\\tif ok {\\n265: \\t\\t\\t\\toldg.stop()\\n266: \\t\\t\\t\\tnewg.CopyState(oldg)\\n267: \\t\\t\\t}\\n268: \\t\\t\\twg.Done()\\n269: \\t\\t\\t// Wait with starting evaluation until the rule manager\\n270: \\t\\t\\t// is told to run. This is necessary to avoid running\\n271: \\t\\t\\t// queries against a bootstrapping storage.\\n272: \\t\\t\\t<-m.block\\n273: \\t\\t\\tnewg.run(m.opts.Context)\\n274: \\t\\t}(newg)\\n275: \\t}\\n276: \\n277: \\t// Stop remaining old groups.\\n278: \\twg.Add(len(m.groups))\\n279: \\tfor n, oldg := range m.groups {\\n280: \\t\\tgo func(n string, g *Group) {\\n281: \\t\\t\\tg.markStale = true\\n282: \\t\\t\\tg.stop()\\n283: \\t\\t\\tif m := g.metrics; m != nil {\\n284: \\t\\t\\t\\tm.IterationsMissed.DeleteLabelValues(n)\\n285: \\t\\t\\t\\tm.IterationsScheduled.DeleteLabelValues(n)\\n286: \\t\\t\\t\\tm.EvalTotal.DeleteLabelValues(n)\\n287: \\t\\t\\t\\tm.EvalFailures.DeleteLabelValues(n)\\n288: \\t\\t\\t\\tm.GroupInterval.DeleteLabelValues(n)\\n289: \\t\\t\\t\\tm.GroupLastEvalTime.DeleteLabelValues(n)\\n290: \\t\\t\\t\\tm.GroupLastDuration.DeleteLabelValues(n)\\n291: \\t\\t\\t\\tm.GroupRules.DeleteLabelValues(n)\\n292: \\t\\t\\t\\tm.GroupSamples.DeleteLabelValues((n))\\n293: \\t\\t\\t}\\n294: \\t\\t\\twg.Done()\\n295: \\t\\t}(n, oldg)\\n296: \\t}\\n297: \\n298: \\twg.Wait()\\n299: \\tm.groups = groups\\n300: \\n301: \\treturn nil\\n302: }\\n303: \\n304: // GroupLoader is responsible for loading rule groups from arbitrary sources and parsing them.\\n305: type GroupLoader interface {\\n306: \\tLoad(identifier string, ignoreUnknownFields bool, nameValidationScheme model.ValidationScheme) (*rulefmt.RuleGroups, []error)\\n307: \\tParse(query string) (parser.Expr, error)\\n308: }\\n309: \\n310: // FileLoader is the default GroupLoader implementation. It defers to rulefmt.ParseFile\\n311: // and parser.ParseExpr.\\n312: type FileLoader struct{}\\n313: \\n314: func (FileLoader) Load(identifier string, ignoreUnknownFields bool, nameValidationScheme model.ValidationScheme) (*rulefmt.RuleGroups, []error) {\\n315: \\treturn rulefmt.ParseFile(identifier, ignoreUnknownFields, nameValidationScheme)\\n316: }\\n317: \\n318: func (FileLoader) Parse(query string) (parser.Expr, error) { return parser.ParseExpr(query) }\\n319: \\n320: // LoadGroups reads groups from a list of files.\\n321: func (m *Manager) LoadGroups(\\n322: \\tinterval time.Duration, externalLabels labels.Labels, externalURL string, groupEvalIterationFunc GroupEvalIterationFunc, ignoreUnknownFields bool, filenames ...string,\\n323: ) (map[string]*Group, []error) {\\n324: \\tgroups m.opts.GroupLoader.Load(fn, ignoreUnknownFields, m.opts.NameValidationScheme)\\n330: \\t\\tif errs != nil {\\n331: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, errs\\n332: \\t\\t}\\n333: \\n334: \\t\\tfor _, rg := range rgs.Groups {\\n335: \\t\\t\\titv := interval\\n336: \\t\\t\\tif rg.Interval != 0 {\\n337: \\t\\t\\t\\titv = time.Duration(rg.Interval)\\n338: \\t\\t\\t}\\n339: \\n340: \\t\\t\\trules := make([]Rule, If PromQL engine crashes while executing any query, such query should be present\n// in the tracker on restart, hence logged. After the logging on restart, the tracker gets emptied.\n//\n// 2) Enforcement of the maximum number of concurrent queries.\ntype QueryTracker interface {\n\tio.Closer\n\n\t// GetMaxConcurrent returns maximum number of concurrent queries that are allowed by this tracker.\n\tGetMaxConcurrent() int\n\n\t// Insert inserts query into query tracker. This call must block if maximum number of queries is already running.\n\t// If Insert doesn't return error then returned integer value should be used in subsequent Delete call.\n\t// Insert should return error if context is finished before query can proceed, and integer value returned in this case should be ignored by caller.\n\tInsert(ctx context.Context, query string) (int, error)\n\n\t// Delete removes query from activity tracker.
