\n117: // QueryEngine defines the interface for the *promql.Engine, so it can be replaced, wrapped or mocked.\n118: type QueryEngine interface {\n119: \tNewInstantQuery(ctx context.Context, q storage.Queryable, opts QueryOpts, qs string, ts time.Time) (Query, error)\n120: \tNewRangeQuery(ctx context.Context, q storage.Queryable, opts QueryOpts, qs string, start, end time.Time, interval time.Duration) (Query, error)\n121: }\n122: \n123: var _ QueryLogger = (*logging.JSONFileLogger)(nil)\n124: \n125: // QueryLogger is an interface that can be used to log all the queries logged\n126: // by the engine.\n127: type QueryLogger interface {\n128: \tslog.Handler\n129: \tio.Closer\n130: }\n131: \n132: // A Query is derived from a raw query string and can be run against an engine\n133: // it is associated with.\n134: type Query interface {\n135: \t// Exec processes the query. Can only be called once.\n136: \tExec(ctx context.Context) *Result\n137: \t// Close recovers memory used by the query result.\n138: \tClose()\n139: \t// Statement returns the parsed statement of the query.\n140: \tStatement() parser.Statement\n141: \t// Stats returns statistics about the lifetime of the query.\n142: \tStats() *stats.Statistics\n143: \t// Cancel signals that a running query execution should be aborted.\n144: \tCancel()\n145: \t// String returns the original query string.\n146: \tString() string\n147: }\n148: \n149: type PrometheusQueryOpts struct {\n150: \t// Enables recording per-step statistics if the engine has it enabled as well. Disabled by default.\n151: \tenablePerStepStats bool\n152: \t// Lookback delta duration for this query.\n153: \tlookbackDelta time.Duration\n154: }\n155: \n156: var _ QueryOpts = &PrometheusQueryOpts{}\n157: \n158: func NewPrometheusQueryOpts(enablePerStepStats bool, lookbackDelta time.Duration) QueryOpts {\n159: \treturn &PrometheusQueryOpts{\n160: \t\tenablePerStepStats: enablePerStepStats,\n161: \t\tlookbackDelta:      lookbackDelta,\n162: \t}\n163: }\n164: \n165: func (p *PrometheusQueryOpts) EnablePerStepStats() bool {\n166: \treturn p.enablePerStepStats\n167: }\n168: \n169: func (p *PrometheusQueryOpts) LookbackDelta() time.Duration {\n170: \treturn p.lookbackDelta\n171: }\n172: \n173: type QueryOpts interface {\n174: \t// Enables recording per-step statistics if the engine has it enabled as well. Disabled by default.\n175: \tEnablePerStepStats() bool\n176: \t// Lookback delta duration for this query.\n177: \tLookbackDelta() time.Duration\n178: }\n179: \n180: // query implements the Query interface.\n181: type query struct {\n182: \t// Underlying data provider.\n183: \tqueryable storage.Queryable\n184: \t// The original query string.\n185: \tq string\n186: \t// Statement of the parsed query.\n187: \tstmt parser.Statement\n188: \t// Timer stats for the query execution.\n189: \tstats *stats.QueryTimers\n190: \t// Sample stats for the query execution.\n191: \tsampleStats *stats.QuerySamples\n192: \t// Result matrix for reuse.\n193: \tmatrix Matrix\n194: \t// Cancellation function for the query.\n195: \tcancel func()\n196: \n197: \t// The engine against which the query is executed.\n198: \tng *Engine\n199: }\n200: \n201: type QueryOrigin struct{}\n202: \n203: // Statement implements the Query interface.\n204: // Calling this after Exec may result in panic,\n205: // see https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/issues/8949.\n206: func (q *query) Statement() parser.Statement {\n207: \treturn q.stmt\n208: }\n209: \n210: // String implements the Query interface.\n211: func (q *query) String() string {\n212: \treturn q.q\n213: }\n214: \n215: // Stats implements the Query interface.\n216: func (q *query) Stats() *stats.Statistics {\n217: \treturn &stats.Statistics{\n218: \t\tTimers:  q.stats,\n219: \t\tSamples: q.sampleStats,\n220: \t}\n221: }\n222: \n223: // Cancel implements the Query interface.\n224: func (q *query) Cancel() {\n225: \tif q.cancel != nil {\n226: \t\tq.cancel()\n227: \t}\n228: }\n229: \n230: // Close implements the Query interface.\n231: func (q *query) Close() {\n232: \tfor _, s := range q.matrix {\n233: \t\tputFPointSlice(s.Floats)\n234: \t\tputHPointSlice(s.Histograms)\n235: \t}\n236: }\n237: \n238: // Exec implements the Query interface.\n239: func (q *query) Exec(ctx context.Context) *Result {\n240: \tif span := trace.SpanFromContext(ctx); span != nil {\n241: \t\tspan.SetAttributes(attribute.String(queryTag, q.stmt.String()))\n242: \t}\n243: \n244: \t// Exec query.\n245: \tres, warnings, err := q.ng.exec(ctx, q)\n246: \treturn &Result{Err: err, Value: res, Warnings: warnings}\n247: }\n248: \n249: // contextDone returns an error if the context was canceled or timed out.\n250: func contextDone(ctx context.Context, env string) error {\n251: \tif err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {\n252: \t\treturn contextErr(err, env)\n253: \t}\n254: \treturn nil\n255: }\n256: \n257: func contextErr(err error, env string) error {\n258: \tswitch {\n259: \tcase errors.Is(err, context.Canceled):\n260: \t\treturn ErrQueryCanceled(env)\n261: \tcase errors.Is(err, context.DeadlineExceeded):\n262: \t\treturn ErrQueryTimeout(env)\n263: \tdefault:\n264: \t\treturn err\n265: \t}\n266: }\n267: \n268: // QueryTracker provides access to two features:\n269: //\n270: // 1) Tracking of active query. If PromQL engine crashes while executing any query, such query should be present\n271: // in the tracker on restart, hence logged. After the logging on restart, the tracker gets emptied.\n272: //\n273: // 2) Enforcement of the maximum number of concurrent queries.\n274: type QueryTracker interface {\n275: \tio.Closer\n276: \n277: \t// GetMaxConcurrent returns maximum number of concurrent queries that are allowed by this tracker.\n278: \tGetMaxConcurrent() int\n279: \n280: \t// Insert inserts query into query tracker. This call must block if maximum number of queries is already running.\n281: \t// If Insert doesn't return error then returned integer value should be used in subsequent Delete call.\n282: \t// Insert should return error if context is finished before query can proceed, and integer value returned in this case should be ignored by caller.\n283: \tInsert(ctx context.Context, query string) (int, error)\n284: \n285: \t// Delete removes query from activity tracker. InsertIndex is value returned by Insert call.\n286: \tDelete(insertIndex int)\n287: }\n288: \n289: // EngineOpts contains configuration options used when creating a new Engine.\n290: type EngineOpts struct {\n291: \tLogger             *slog.Logger\n292: \tReg                prometheus.Registerer\n293: \tMaxSamples         int\n294: \tTimeout            time.Duration\n295: \tActiveQueryTracker QueryTracker\n296: \t// LookbackDelta determines the time since the last sample after which a time\n297: \t// series is considered stale.\n298: \tLookbackDelta time.Duration\n299: \n300: \t// NoStepSubqueryIntervalFn is the default evaluation interval of\n301: \t// a subquery in milliseconds if no step in range vector was specified `[30m:<step>]`.\n302: \tNoStepSubqueryIntervalFn func(rangeMillis int64) int64\n303: \n304: \t// EnableAtModifier if true enables @ modifier. Disabled otherwise. This\n305: \t// is supposed to be enabled for regular PromQL (as of Prometheus v2.33)\n306: \t// but the option to disable it is still provided here for those using\n307: \t// the Engine outside of Prometheus.\n308: \tEnableAtModifier bool\n309: \n310: \t// EnableNegativeOffset if true enables negative (-) offset\n311: \t// values. Disabled otherwise. This is supposed to be enabled for\n312: \t// regular PromQL (as of Prometheus v2.33) but the option to disable it\n313: \t// is still provided here for those using the Engine outside of\n314: \t// Prometheus.\n315: \tEnableNegativeOffset bool\n316: \n317: \t// EnablePerStepStats if true allows for per-step stats to be computed on request. Disabled otherwise.\n318: \tEnablePerStepStats bool\n319: \n320: \t// EnableDelayedNameRemoval delays the removal of the __name__ label to the last step of the query evaluation.\n321: \t// This is useful in certain scenarios where the __name__ label must be preserved or where applying a\n322: \t// regex-matcher to the __name__ label may otherwise lead to duplicate labelset errors.\n323: \tEnableDelayedNameRemoval bool\n324: \t// EnableTypeAndUnitLabels will allow PromQL Engine to make decisions based on the type and unit labels.\n325: \tEnableTypeAndUnitLabels bool\n326: }\n327: \n328: // Engine handles the lifetime of queries from beginning to end.\n329: // It is connected to a querier.\n330: type Engine struct {\n331: \tlogger                   *slog.Logger\n332: \tmetrics                  *engineMetrics\n333: \ttimeout                  time.Duration\n334: \tmaxSamplesPerQuery       int\n335: \tactiveQueryTracker       QueryTracker\n336: \tqueryLogger              QueryLogger\n337: \tqueryLoggerLock          sync.RWMutex\n338: \tlookbackDelta            time.Duration\n339: \tnoStepSubqueryIntervalFn func(rangeMillis int64) int64\n340: \tenableAtModifier         bool\n341: \tenableNegativeOffset     bool\n342: \tenablePerStepStats       bool\n343: \tenableDelayedNameRemoval bool\n344: \tenableTypeAndUnitLabels  bool\n345: }\n346: \n347: // NewEngine returns a new engine.\n348: func NewEngine(opts EngineOpts) *Engine {\n349: \tif opts.Logger == nil {\n350: \t\topts.Logger = promslog.NewNopLogger()\n351: \t}\n352: \n353: \tqueryResultSummary := prometheus.NewSummaryVec(prometheus.SummaryOpts{\n354: \t\tNamespace:  namespace,\n355: \t\tSubsystem:  subsystem,\n356: \t\tName:       \"query_duration_seconds\",\n357: \t\tHelp:       \"Query timings\",\n358: \t\tObjectives: map[float64]float64{0.5: 0.05, 0.9: 0.01, 0.99: 0.001},\n359: \t},\n360: \t\t[]string{\"slice\"},\n361: \t)\n362: \n363: \tmetrics := &engineMetrics{\n364: \t\tcurrentQueries: prometheus.NewGauge(prometheus.GaugeOpts{\n365: \t\t\tNamespace: namespace,\n366: \t\t\tSubsystem: subsystem,\n367: \t\t\tName:      \"queries\",\n368: \t\t\tHelp:      \"The current number of queries being executed or waiting.\",\n369: \t\t}),\n370: \t\tqueryLogEnabled: prometheus.NewGauge(prometheus.GaugeOpts{\n371: \t\t\tNamespace: namespace,\n372: \t\t\tSubsystem: subsystem,\n373: \t\t\tName:      \"query_log_enabled\",\n374: \t\t\tHelp:      \"State of the query log.\",\n375: \t\t}),\n376: \t\tqueryLogFailures: prometheus.NewCounter(prometheus.CounterOpts{\n377: \t\t\tNamespace: namespace,\n378: \t\t\tSubsystem: subsystem,\n379: \t\t\tName:      \"query_log_failures_total\",\n380: \t\t\tHelp:      \"The number of query log failures.\",\n381: \t\t}),\n382: \t\tmaxConcurrentQueries: prometheus.NewGauge(prometheus.GaugeOpts{\n383: \t\t\tNamespace: namespace,\n384: \t\t\tSubsystem: subsystem,\n385: \t\t\tName:      \"queries_concurrent_max\",\n386: \t\t\tHelp:      \"The max number of concurrent queries.\",\n387: \t\t}),\n388: \t\tquerySamples: prometheus.NewCounter(prometheus.CounterOpts{\n389: \t\t\tNamespace: namespace,\n390: \t\t\tSubsystem: subsystem,\n391: \t\t\tName:      \"query_samples_total\",\n392: \t\t\tHelp:      \"The total number of samples loaded by all queries.\",\n393: \t\t}),\n394: \t\tqueryQueueTime:   queryResultSummary.WithLabelValues(\"queue_time\"),\n395: \t\tqueryPrepareTime: queryResultSummary.WithLabelValues(\"prepare_time\"),\n396: \t\tqueryInnerEval:   queryResultSummary.WithLabelValues(\"inner_eval\"),\n397: \t\tqueryResultSort:  queryResultSummary.WithLabelValues(\"result_sort\"),\n398: \t}\n399: \n400: \tif t := opts.ActiveQueryTracker; t != nil {\n401: \t\tmetrics.maxConcurrentQueries.Set(float64(t.GetMaxConcurrent()))\n402: \t} else {\n403: \t\tmetrics.maxConcurrentQueries.Set(-1)\n404: \t}\n405: \n406: \tif opts.LookbackDelta == 0 {\n407: \t\topts.LookbackDelta = defaultLookbackDelta\n408: \t\tif l := opts.Logger; l != nil {\n409: \t\t\tl.Debug(\"Lookback delta is zero, setting to default value\", \"value\", defaultLookbackDelta)\n410: \t\t}\n411: \t}\n412: \n413: \tif opts.Reg != nil {\n414: \t\topts.Reg.MustRegister(\n415: \t\t\tmetrics.currentQueries,\n416: \t\t\tmetrics.maxConcurrentQueries,\n417: \t\t\tmetrics.queryLogEnabled,\n418: \t\t\tmetrics.queryLogFailures,\n419: \t\t\tmetrics.querySamples,\n420: \t\t\tqueryResultSummary,\n421: \t\t)\n422: \t}\n423: \n424: \treturn &Engine{\n425: \t\ttimeout:                  opts.Timeout,\n426: \t\tlogger:                   opts.Logger,\n427: \t\tmetrics:                  metrics,\n428: \t\tmaxSamplesPerQuery:       opts.MaxSamples,\n429: \t\tactiveQueryTracker:       opts.ActiveQueryTracker,\n430: \t\tlookbackDelta:            opts.LookbackDelta,\n431: \t\tnoStepSubqueryIntervalFn: opts.NoStepSubqueryIntervalFn,\n432: \t\tenableAtModifier:         opts.EnableAtModifier,\n433: \t\tenableNegativeOffset:     opts.EnableNegativeOffset,\n434: \t\tenablePerStepStats:       opts.EnablePerStepStats,\n435: \t\tenableDelayedNameRemoval: opts.EnableDelayedNameRemoval,\n436: \t\tenableTypeAndUnitLabels:  opts.EnableTypeAndUnitLabels,\n437: \t}\n438: }\n439: \n440: // Close closes ng.\n441: // Callers must ensure the engine is really no longer in use before calling this to avoid\n442: // issues failures like in https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/issues/15232\n443: func (ng *Engine) Close() error {\n444: \tif ng == nil {\n445: \t\treturn nil\n446: \t}\n447: \n448: \tif ng.activeQueryTracker != nil {\n449: \t\treturn ng.activeQueryTracker.Close()\n450: \t}\n451: \treturn nil\n452: }\n453: \n454: // SetQueryLogger sets the query logger.\n455: func (ng *Engine) SetQueryLogger(l QueryLogger) {\n456: \tng.queryLoggerLock.Lock()\n457: \tdefer ng.queryLoggerLock.Unlock()\n458: \n459: \tif ng.queryLogger != nil {\n460: \t\t// An error closing the old file descriptor should\n461: \t\t// not make reload fail; only log a warning.\n462: \t\terr := ng.queryLogger.Close()\n463: \t\tif err != nil {\n464: \t\t\tng.logger.Warn(\"Error while closing the previous query log file\", \"err\", err)\n465: \t\t}\n466: \t}\n467: \n468: \tng.queryLogger = l\n469: \n470: \tif l != nil {\n471: \t\tng.metrics.queryLogEnabled.Set(1)\n472: \t} else {\n473: \t\tng.metrics.queryLogEnabled.Set(0)\n474: \t}\n475: }\n476: \n477: // NewInstantQuery returns an evaluation query for the given expression at the given time.\n478: func (ng *Engine) NewInstantQuery(ctx context.Context, q storage.Queryable, opts QueryOpts, qs string, ts time.Time) (Query, error) {\n479: \tpExpr, qry := ng.newQuery(q, qs, opts, ts, ts, 0*time.Second)\n480: \tfinishQueue, err := ng.queueActive(ctx, qry)\n481: \tif err != nil {\n482: \t\treturn nil, err\n483: \t}\n484: \tdefer finishQueue()\n485: \texpr, err := parser.ParseExpr(qs)\n486: \tif err != nil {\n487: \t\treturn nil, err\n488: \t}\n489: \tif err := ng.validateOpts(expr); err != nil {\n490: \t\treturn nil, err\n491: \t}\n492: \t*pExpr, err = PreprocessExpr(expr, ts, ts, 0)\n493: \n494: \treturn qry, err\n495: }\n496: \n497: // NewRangeQuery returns an evaluation query for the given time range and with\n498: // the resolution set by the interval.\n499: func (ng *Engine) NewRangeQuery(ctx context.Context, q storage.Queryable, opts QueryOpts, qs string, start, end time.Time, interval time.Duration) (Query, error) {\n500: \tpExpr, qry := ng.newQuery(q, qs, opts, start, end, interval)\n501: \tfinishQueue, err := ng.queueActive(ctx, qry)\n502: \tif err != nil {\n503: \t\treturn nil, err\n504: \t}\n505: \tdefer finishQueue()\n506: \texpr, err := parser.ParseExpr(qs)\n507: \tif err != nil {\n508: \t\treturn nil, err\n509: \t}\n510: \tif err := ng.validateOpts(expr); err != nil {\n511: \t\treturn nil, err\n512: \t}\n513: \tif expr.Type() != parser.ValueTypeVector && expr.Type() != parser.ValueTypeScalar {\n514: \t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid expression type %q for range query, must be Scalar or instant Vector\", parser.DocumentedType(expr.Type()))\n515: \t}\n516: \t*pExpr, err = PreprocessExpr(expr, start, end, interval)\n517: \n518: \treturn qry, err\n519: }\n520: \n521: func (ng *Engine) newQuery(q storage.Queryable, qs string, opts QueryOpts, start, end time.Time, interval time.Duration) (*parser.Expr, *query) {\n522: \tif opts == nil {\n523: \t\topts = NewPrometheusQueryOpts(false, 0)\n524: \t}\n525: \n526: \tlookbackDelta := opts.LookbackDelta()\n527: \tif lookbackDelta <= 0 {\n528: \t\tlookbackDelta = ng.lookbackDelta\n529: \t}\n530: \n531: \tes := &parser.EvalStmt{\n532: \t\tStart:         start,\n533: \t\tEnd:           end,\n534: \t\tInterval:      interval,\n535: \t\tLookbackDelta: lookbackDelta,\n536: \t}\n537: \tqry := &query{\n538: \t\tq:           qs,\n539: \t\tstmt:        es,\n540: \t\tng:          ng,\n541: \t\tstats:       stats.NewQueryTimers(),\n542: \t\tsampleStats: stats.NewQuerySamples(ng.enablePerStepStats && opts.EnablePerStepStats()),\n543: \t\tqueryable:   q,\n544: \t}\n545: \treturn &es.Expr, qry\n546: }\n547: \n548: var (\n549: \tErrValidationAtModifierDisabled     = errors.New(\"@ modifier is disabled\")\n550: \tErrValidationNegativeOffsetDisabled = errors.New(\"negative offset is disabled\")\n551: )\n552: \n553: func (ng *Engine) validateOpts(expr parser.Expr) error {\n554: \tif ng.enableAtModifier && ng.enableNegativeOffset {\n555: \t\treturn nil\n556: \t}\n557: \n558: \tvar atModifierUsed, negativeOffsetUsed bool\n559: \n560: \tvar validationErr error\n561: \tparser.Inspect(expr, func(node parser.Node, _ []parser.Node) error {\n562: \t\tswitch n := node.(type) {\n563: \t\tcase *parser.VectorSelector:\n564: \t\t\tif n.Timestamp != nil || n.StartOrEnd == parser.START || n.StartOrEnd == parser.END {\n565: \t\t\t\tatModifierUsed = true\n566: \t\t\t}\n567: \t\t\tif n.OriginalOffset < 0 {\n568: \t\t\t\tnegativeOffsetUsed = true\n569: \t\t\t}\n570: \n571: \t\tcase *parser.MatrixSelector:\n572: \t\t\tvs := n.VectorSelector.(*parser.VectorSelector)\n573: \t\t\tif vs.Timestamp != nil || vs.StartOrEnd == parser.START || vs.StartOrEnd == parser.END {\n574: \t\t\t\tatModifierUsed = true\n575: \t\t\t}\n576: \t\t\tif vs.OriginalOffset < 0 {\n577: \t\t\t\tnegativeOffsetUsed = true\n578: \t\t\t}\n579: \n580: \t\tcase *parser.SubqueryExpr:\n581: \t\t\tif n.Timestamp != nil || n.StartOrEnd == parser.START || n.StartOrEnd == parser.END {\n582: \t\t\t\tatModifierUsed = true\n583: \t\t\t}\n584: \t\t\tif n.OriginalOffset < 0 {\n585: \t\t\t\tnegativeOffsetUsed = true\n586: \t\t\t}\n587: \t\t}\n588: \n589: \t\tif atModifierUsed && !ng.enableAtModifier {\n590: \t\t\tvalidationErr = ErrValidationAtModifierDisabled\n591: \t\t\treturn validationErr\n592: \t\t}\n593: \t\tif negativeOffsetUsed && !ng.enableNegativeOffset {\n594: \t\t\tvalidationErr = ErrValidationNegativeOffsetDisabled\n595: \t\t\treturn validationErr\n596: \t\t}\n597: \n598: \t\treturn nil\n599: \t})\n600: \n601: \treturn validationErr\n602: }\n603: \n604: // NewTestQuery injects special behaviour into Query for testing.\n605: func (ng *Engine) NewTestQuery(f func(context.Context) error) Query {\n606: \tqry := &query{\n607: \t\tq:           \"test statement\",\n608: \t\tstmt:        parser.TestStmt(f),\n609: \t\tng:          ng,\n610: \t\tstats:       stats.NewQueryTimers(),\n611: \t\tsampleStats: stats.NewQuerySamples(ng.enablePerStepStats),\n612: \t}\n613: \treturn qry\n614: }\n615: \n616: // exec executes the query.\n617: //\n618: // At this point per query only one EvalStmt is evaluated. Alert and record\n619: // statements are not handled by the Engine.\n620: func (ng *Engine) exec(ctx context.Context, q *query) (v parser.Value, ws annotations.Annotations, err error) {\n621: \tng.metrics.currentQueries.Inc()\n622: \tdefer func() {\n623: \t\tng.metrics.currentQueries.Dec()\n624: \t\tng.metrics.querySamples.Add(float64(q.sampleStats.TotalSamples))\n625: \t}()\n626: \n627: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, ng.timeout)\n628: \tq.cancel = cancel\n629: \n630: \tdefer func() {\n631: \t\tng.queryLoggerLock.RLock()\n632: \t\tif l := ng.queryLogger; l != nil {\n633: \t\t\tlogger := slog.New(l)\n634: \t\t\tf := make([]slog.Attr, 0, 16) // Probably enough up front to not need to reallocate on append.\n635: \n636: \t\t\tparams := make(map[string]any, 4)\n637: \t\t\tparams[\"query\"] = q.q\n638: \t\t\tif eq, ok := q.Statement().(*parser.EvalStmt); ok {\n639: \t\t\t\tparams[\"start\"] = formatDate(eq.Start)\n640: \t\t\t\tparams[\"end\"] = formatDate(eq.End)\n641: \t\t\t\t// The step provided by the user is in seconds.\n642: \t\t\t\tparams[\"step\"] = int64(eq.Interval / (time.Second / time.Nanosecond))\n643: \t\t\t}\n644: \t\t\tf = append(f, slog.Any(\"params\", params))\n645: \t\t\tif err != nil {\n646: \t\t\t\tf = append(f, slog.Any(\"error\", err))\n647: \t\t\t}\n648: \t\t\tf = append(f, slog.Any(\"stats\", stats.NewQueryStats(q.Stats())))\n649: \t\t\tif span := trace.SpanFromContext(ctx); span != nil {\n650: \t\t\t\tf = append(f, slog.Any(\"spanID\", span.SpanContext().SpanID()))\n651: \t\t\t}\n652: \t\t\tif origin := ctx.Value(QueryOrigin{}); origin != nil {\n653: \t\t\t\tfor k, v := range origin.(map[string]any) {\n654: \t\t\t\t\tf = append(f, slog.Any(k, v))\n655: \t\t\t\t}\n656: \t\t\t}\n657: \t\t\tlogger.LogAttrs(context.Background(), slog.LevelInfo, \"promql query logged\", f...)\n658: \t\t\t// TODO: @tjhop -- do we still need this metric/error log if logger doesn't return errors?\n659: \t\t\t// ng.metrics.queryLogFailures.Inc()\n660: \t\t\t// ng.logger.Error(\"can't log query\", \"err\", err)\n661: \t\t}\n662: \t\tng.queryLoggerLock.RUnlock()\n663: \t}()\n664: \n665: \texecSpanTimer, ctx := q.stats.GetSpanTimer(ctx, stats.ExecTotalTime)\n666: \tdefer execSpanTimer.Finish()\n667: \n668: \tfinishQueue, err := ng.queueActive(ctx, q)\n669: \tif err != nil {\n670: \t\treturn nil, nil, err\n671: \t}\n672: \tdefer finishQueue()\n673: \n674: \t// Cancel when execution is done or an error was raised.\n675: \tdefer q.cancel()\n676: \n677: \tevalSpanTimer, ctx := q.stats.GetSpanTimer(ctx, stats.EvalTotalTime)\n678: \tdefer evalSpanTimer.Finish()\n679: \n680: \t// The base context might already be canceled on the first iteration (e.g. during shutdown).\n681: \tif err := contextDone(ctx, env); err != nil {\n682: \t\treturn nil, nil, err\n683: \t}\n684: \n685: \tswitch s := q.Statement().(type) {\n686: \tcase *parser.EvalStmt:\n687: \t\treturn ng.execEvalStmt(ctx, q, s)\n688: \tcase parser.TestStmt:\n689: \t\treturn nil, nil, s(ctx)\n690: \t}\n691: \n692: \tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\"promql.Engine.exec: unhandled statement of type %T\", q.Statement()))\n693: }\n694: \n695: // Log query in active log. The active log guarantees that we don't run over\n696: // MaxConcurrent queries.\n697: func (ng *Engine) queueActive(ctx context.Context, q *query) (func(), error) {\n698: \tif ng.activeQueryTracker == nil {\n699: \t\treturn func() {}, nil\n700: \t}\n701: \tqueueSpanTimer, _ := q.stats.GetSpanTimer(ctx, stats.ExecQueueTime, ng.metrics.queryQueueTime)\n702: \tqueryIndex, err := ng.activeQueryTracker.Insert(ctx, q.q)\n703: \tqueueSpanTimer.Finish()\n704: \treturn func() { ng.activeQueryTracker.Delete(queryIndex) }, err\n705: }\n706: \n707: func timeMilliseconds(t time.Time) int64 {\n708: \treturn t.UnixNano() / int64(time.Millisecond/time.Nanosecond)\n709: }\n710: \n711: func durationMilliseconds(d time.Duration) int64 {\n712: \treturn int64(d / (time.Millisecond / time.Nanosecond))\n713: }\n714: \n715: // execEvalStmt evaluates the expression of an evaluation statement for the given time range.\n716: func (ng *Engine) execEvalStmt(ctx context.Context, query *query, s *parser.EvalStmt) (parser.Value, annotations.Annotations, error) {\n717: \tprepareSpanTimer, ctxPrepare := query.stats.GetSpanTimer(ctx, stats.QueryPreparationTime, ng.metrics.queryPrepareTime)\n718: \tmint, maxt := FindMinMaxTime(s)\n719: \tquerier, err := query.queryable.Querier(mint, maxt)\n720: \tif err != nil {\n721: \t\tprepareSpanTimer.Finish()\n722: \t\treturn nil, nil, err\n723: \t}\n724: \tdefer querier.Close()\n725: \n726: \tng.populateSeries(ctxPrepare, querier, s)\n727: \tprepareSpanTimer.Finish()\n728: \n729: \t// Modify the offset of vector and matrix selectors for the @ modifier\n730: \t// w.r.t. the start time since only 1 evaluation will be done on them.\n731: \tsetOffsetForAtModifier(timeMilliseconds(s.Start), s.Expr)\n732: \tevalSpanTimer, ctxInnerEval := query.stats.GetSpanTimer(ctx, stats.InnerEvalTime, ng.metrics.queryInnerEval)\n733: \t// Instant evaluation. This is executed as a range evaluation with one step.\n734: \tif s.Start.Equal(s.End) && s.Interval == 0 {\n735: \t\tstart := timeMilliseconds(s.Start)\n736: \t\tevaluator := &evaluator{\n737: \t\t\tstartTimestamp:           start,\n738: \t\t\tendTimestamp:             start,\n739: \t\t\tinterval:                 1,\n740: \t\t\tmaxSamples:               ng.maxSamplesPerQuery,\n741: \t\t\tlogger:                   ng.logger,\n742: \t\t\tlookbackDelta:            s.LookbackDelta,\n743: \t\t\tsamplesStats:             query.sampleStats,\n744: \t\t\tnoStepSubqueryIntervalFn: ng.noStepSubqueryIntervalFn,\n745: \t\t\tenableDelayedNameRemoval: ng.enableDelayedNameRemoval,\n746: \t\t\tenableTypeAndUnitLabels:  ng.enableTypeAndUnitLabels,\n747: \t\t\tquerier:                  querier,\n748: \t\t}\n749: \t\tquery.sampleStats.InitStepTracking(start, start, 1)\n750: \n751: \t\tval, warnings, err := evaluator.Eval(ctxInnerEval, s.Expr)\n752: \n753: \t\tevalSpanTimer.Finish()\n754: \n755: \t\tif err != nil {\n756: \t\t\treturn nil, warnings, err\n757: \t\t}\n758: \n759: \t\tvar mat Matrix\n760: \n761: \t\tswitch result := val.(type) {\n762: \t\tcase Matrix:\n763: \t\t\tmat = result\n764: \t\tcase String:\n765: \t\t\treturn result, warnings, nil\n766: \t\tdefault:\n767: \t\t\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\"promql.Engine.exec: invalid expression type %q\", val.Type()))\n768: \t\t}\n769: \n770: \t\tquery.matrix = mat\n771: \t\tswitch s.Expr.Type() {\n772: \t\tcase parser.ValueTypeVector:\n773: \t\t\t// Convert matrix with one value per series into vector.\n774: \t\t\tvector := make(Vector, len(mat))\n775: \t\t\tfor i, s := range mat {\n776: \t\t\t\t// Point might have a different timestamp, force it to the evaluation\n777: \t\t\t\t// timestamp as that is when we ran the evaluation.\n778: \t\t\t\tif len(s.Histograms) > 0 {\n779: \t\t\t\t\tvector[i] = Sample{Metric: s.Metric, H: s.Histograms[0].H, T: start, DropName: s.DropName}\n780: \t\t\t\t} else {\n781: \t\t\t\t\tvector[i] = Sample{Metric: s.Metric, F: s.Floats[0].F, T: start, DropName: s.DropName}\n782: \t\t\t\t}\n783: \t\t\t}\n784: \t\t\treturn vector, warnings, nil\n785: \t\tcase parser.ValueTypeScalar:\n786: \t\t\treturn Scalar{V: mat[0].Floats[0].F, T: start}, warnings, nil\n787: \t\tcase parser.ValueTypeMatrix:\n788: \t\t\tng.sortMatrixResult(ctx, query, mat)\n789: \t\t\treturn mat, warnings, nil\n790: \t\tdefault:\n791: \t\t\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\"promql.Engine.exec: unexpected expression type %q\", s.Expr.Type()))\n792: \t\t}\n793: \t}\n794: \n795: \t// Range evaluation.\n796: \tevaluator := &evaluator{\n797: \t\tstartTimestamp:           timeMilliseconds(s.Start),\n798: \t\tendTimestamp:             timeMilliseconds(s.End),\n799: \t\tinterval:                 durationMilliseconds(s.Interval),\n800: \t\tmaxSamples:               ng.maxSamplesPerQuery,\n801: \t\tlogger:                   ng.logger,\n802: \t\tlookbackDelta:            s.LookbackDelta,\n803: \t\tsamplesStats:             query.sampleStats,\n804: \t\tnoStepSubqueryIntervalFn: ng.noStepSubqueryIntervalFn,\n805: \t\tenableDelayedNameRemoval: ng.enableDelayedNameRemoval,\n806: \t\tenableTypeAndUnitLabels:  ng.enableTypeAndUnitLabels,\n807: \t\tquerier:                  querier,\n808: \t}\n809: \tquery.sampleStats.InitStepTracking(evaluator.startTimestamp, evaluator.endTimestamp, evaluator.interval)\n810: \tval, warnings, err := evaluator.Eval(ctxInnerEval, s.Expr)\n811: \n812: \tevalSpanTimer.Finish()\n813: \n814: \tif err != nil {\n815: \t\treturn nil, warnings, err\n816: \t}\n817: \n818: \tmat, ok := val.(Matrix)\n819: \tif !ok {\n820: \t\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\"promql.Engine.exec: invalid expression type %q\", val.Type()))\n821: \t}\n822: \tquery.matrix = mat\n823: \n824: \tif err := contextDone(ctx, \"expression evaluation\"); err != nil {\n825: \t\treturn nil, warnings, err\n826: \t}\n827: \n828: \t// TODO(fabxc): where to ensure metric labels are a copy from the storage internals.\n829: \tng.sortMatrixResult(ctx, query, mat)\n830: \n831: \treturn mat, warnings, nil\n832: }\n833: \n834: func (ng *Engine) sortMatrixResult(ctx context.Context, query *query, mat Matrix) {\n835: \tsortSpanTimer, _ := query.stats.GetSpanTimer(ctx, stats.ResultSortTime, ng.metrics.queryResultSort)\n836: \tsort.Sort(mat)\n837: \tsortSpanTimer.Finish()\n838: }\n839: \n840: // subqueryTimes returns the sum of offsets and ranges of all subqueries in the path.\n841: // If the @ modifier is used, then the offset and range is w.r.t. that timestamp\n842: // (i.e. the sum is reset when we have @ modifier).\n843: // The returned *int64 is the closest timestamp that was seen. nil for no @ modifier.\n844: func subqueryTimes(path []parser.Node) (time.Duration, time.Duration, *int64) {\n845: \tvar (\n846: \t\tsubqOffset, subqRange time.Duration\n847: \t\tts                    int64 = math.MaxInt64\n848: \t)\n849: \tfor _, node := range path {\n850: \t\tif n, ok := node.(*parser.SubqueryExpr); ok {\n851: \t\t\tsubqOffset += n.OriginalOffset\n852: \t\t\tsubqRange += n.Range\n853: \t\t\tif n.Timestamp != nil {\n854: \t\t\t\t// The @ modifier on subquery invalidates all the offset and\n855: \t\t\t\t// range till now. Hence resetting it here.\n856: \t\t\t\tsubqOffset = n.OriginalOffset\n857: \t\t\t\tsubqRange = n.Range\n858: \t\t\t\tts = *n.Timestamp\n859: \t\t\t}\n860: \t\t}\n861: \t}\n862: \tvar tsp *int64\n863: \tif ts != math.MaxInt64 {\n864: \t\ttsp = &ts\n865: \t}\n866: \treturn subqOffset, subqRange, tsp\n867: }\n868: \n869: // FindMinMaxTime returns the time in milliseconds of the earliest and latest point in time the statement will try to process.\n870: // This takes into account offsets, @ modifiers, and range selectors.\n871: // If the statement does not select series, then FindMinMaxTime returns (0, 0).\n872: func FindMinMaxTime(s *parser.EvalStmt) (int64, int64) {\n873: \tvar minTimestamp, maxTimestamp int64 = math.MaxInt64, math.MinInt64\n874: \t// Whenever a MatrixSelector is evaluated, evalRange is set to the corresponding range.\n875: \t// The evaluation of the VectorSelector inside then evaluates the given range and unsets\n876: \t// the variable.\n877: \tvar evalRange time.Duration\n878: \tparser.Inspect(s.Expr, func(node parser.Node, path []parser.Node) error {\n879: \t\tswitch n := node.(type) {\n880: \t\tcase *parser.VectorSelector:\n881: \t\t\tstart, end := getTimeRangesForSelector(s, n, path, evalRange)\n882: \t\t\tif start < minTimestamp {\n883: \t\t\t\tminTimestamp = start\n884: \t\t\t}\n885: \t\t\tif end > maxTimestamp {\n886: \t\t\t\tmaxTimestamp = end\n887: \t\t\t}\n888: \t\t\tevalRange = 0\n889: \t\tcase *parser.MatrixSelector:\n890: \t\t\tevalRange = n.Range\n891: \t\t}\n892: \t\treturn nil\n893: \t})\n894: \n895: \tif maxTimestamp == math.MinInt64 {\n896: \t\t// This happens when there was no selector. Hence no time range to select.\n897: \t\tminTimestamp = 0\n898: \t\tmaxTimestamp = 0\n899: \t}\n900: \n901: \treturn minTimestamp, maxTimestamp\n902: }\n903: \n904: func getTimeRangesForSelector(s *parser.EvalStmt, n *parser.VectorSelector, path []parser.Node, evalRange time.Duration) (int64, int64) {\n905: \tstart, end := timestamp.FromTime(s.Start), timestamp.FromTime(s.End)\n906: \tsubqOffset, subqRange, subqTs := subqueryTimes(path)\n907: \n908: \tif subqTs != nil {\n909: \t\t// The timestamp on the subquery overrides the eval statement time ranges.\n910: \t\tstart = *subqTs\n911: \t\tend = *subqTs\n912: \t}\n913: \n914: \tif n.Timestamp != nil {\n915: \t\t// The timestamp on the selector overrides everything.\n916: \t\tstart = *n.Timestamp\n917: \t\tend = *n.Timestamp\n918: \t} else {\n919: \t\toffsetMilliseconds := durationMilliseconds(subqOffset)\n920: \t\tstart = start - offsetMilliseconds - durationMilliseconds(subqRange)\n921: \t\tend -= offsetMilliseconds\n922: \t}\n923: \n924: \tif evalRange == 0 {\n925: \t\t// Reduce the start by one fewer ms than the lookback delta\n926: \t\t// because wo want to exclude samples that are precisely the\n927: \t\t// lookback delta before the eval time.\n928: \t\tstart -= durationMilliseconds(s.LookbackDelta) - 1\n929: \t} else {\n930: \t\t// For all matrix queries we want to ensure that we have\n931: \t\t// (end-start) + range selected this way we have `range` data\n932: \t\t// before the start time. We subtract one from the range to\n933: \t\t// exclude samples positioned directly at the lower boundary of\n934: \t\t// the range.\n935: \t\tstart -= durationMilliseconds(evalRange) - 1\n936: \t}\n937: \n938: \toffsetMilliseconds := durationMilliseconds(n.OriginalOffset)\n939: \tstart -= offsetMilliseconds\n940: \tend -= offsetMilliseconds\n941: \n942: \treturn start, end\n943: }\n944: \n945: func (ng *Engine) getLastSubqueryInterval(path []parser.Node) time.Duration {\n946: \tvar interval time.Duration\n947: \tfor _, node := range path {\n948: \t\tif n, ok := node.(*parser.SubqueryExpr); ok {\n949: \t\t\tinterval = n.Step\n950: \t\t\tif n.Step == 0 {\n951: \t\t\t\tinterval = time.Duration(ng.noStepSubqueryIntervalFn(durationMilliseconds(n.Range))) * time.Millisecond\n952: \t\t\t}\n953: \t\t}\n954: \t}\n955: \treturn interval\n956: }\n957: \n958: func (ng *Engine) populateSeries(ctx context.Context, querier storage.Querier, s *parser.EvalStmt) {\n959: \t// Whenever a MatrixSelector is evaluated, evalRange is set to the corresponding range.\n960: \t// The evaluation of the VectorSelector inside then evaluates the given range and unsets\n961: \t// the variable.\n962: \tvar evalRange time.Duration\n963: \n964: \tparser.Inspect(s.Expr, func(node parser.Node, path []parser.Node) error {\n965: \t\tswitch n := node.(type) {\n966: \t\tcase *parser.VectorSelector:\n967: \t\t\tstart, end := getTimeRangesForSelector(s, n, path, evalRange)\n968: \t\t\tinterval := ng.getLastSubqueryInterval(path)\n969: \t\t\tif interval == 0 {\n970: \t\t\t\tinterval = s.Interval\n971: \t\t\t}\n972: \t\t\thints := &storage.SelectHints{\n973: \t\t\t\tStart: start,\n974: \t\t\t\tEnd:   end,\n975: \t\t\t\tStep:  durationMilliseconds(interval),\n976: \t\t\t\tRange: durationMilliseconds(evalRange),\n977: \t\t\t\tFunc:  extractFuncFromPath(path),\n978: \t\t\t}\n979: \t\t\tevalRange = 0\n980: \t\t\thints.By, hints.Grouping = extractGroupsFromPath(path)\n981: \t\t\tn.UnexpandedSeriesSet = querier.Select(ctx, false, hints, n.LabelMatchers...)\n982: \n983: \t\tcase *parser.MatrixSelector:\n984: \t\t\tevalRange = n.Range\n985: \t\t}\n986: \t\treturn nil\n987: \t})\n988: }\n989: \n990: // extractFuncFromPath walks up the path and searches for the first instance of\n991: // a function or aggregation.\n992: func extractFuncFromPath(p []parser.Node) string {\n993: \tif len(p) == 0 {\n994: \t\treturn \"\"\n995: \t}\n996: \tswitch n := p[len(p)-1].(type) {\n997: \tcase *parser.AggregateExpr:\n998: \t\treturn n.Op.String()\n999: \tcase *parser.Call:\n1000: \t\treturn n.Func.Name\n1001: \tcase *parser.BinaryExpr:\n1002: \t\t// If we hit a binary expression we terminate since we only care about functions\n1003: \t\t// or aggregations over a single metric.\n1004: \t\treturn \"\"\n1005: \t}\n1006: \treturn extractFuncFromPath(p[:len(p)-1])\n1007: }\n1008: \n1009: // extractGroupsFromPath parses vector outer function and extracts grouping information if by or without was used.\n1010: func extractGroupsFromPath(p []parser.Node) (bool, []string) {\n1011: \tif len(p) == 0 {\n1012: \t\treturn false, nil\n1013: \t}\n1014: \tif n, ok := p[len(p)-1].(*parser.AggregateExpr); ok {\n1015: \t\treturn !n.Without, n.Grouping\n1016: \t}\n1017: \treturn false, nil\n1018: }\n1019: \n1020: // checkAndExpandSeriesSet expands expr's UnexpandedSeriesSet into expr's Series.\n1021: // If the Series field is already non-nil, it's a no-op.\n1022: func checkAndExpandSeriesSet(ctx context.Context, expr parser.Expr) (annotations.Annotations, error) {\n1023: \tswitch e := expr.(type) {\n1024: \tcase *parser.MatrixSelector:\n1025: \t\treturn checkAndExpandSeriesSet(ctx, e.VectorSelector)\n1026: \tcase *parser.VectorSelector:\n1027: \t\tif e.Series != nil {\n1028: \t\t\treturn nil, nil\n1029: \t\t}\n1030: \t\tspan := trace.SpanFromContext(ctx)\n1031: \t\tspan.AddEvent(\"expand start\", trace.WithAttributes(attribute.String(\"selector\", e.String())))\n1032: \t\tseries, ws, err := expandSeriesSet(ctx, e.UnexpandedSeriesSet)\n1033: \t\tif e.SkipHistogramBuckets {\n1034: \t\t\tfor i := range series {\n1035: \t\t\t\tseries[i] = newHistogramStatsSeries(series[i])\n1036: \t\t\t}\n1037: \t\t}\n1038: \t\te.Series = series\n1039: \t\tspan.AddEvent(\"expand end\", trace.WithAttributes(attribute.Int(\"num_series\", len(series))))\n1040: \t\treturn ws, err\n1041: \t}\n1042: \treturn nil, nil\n1043: }\n1044: \n1045: func expandSeriesSet(ctx context.Context, it storage.SeriesSet) (res []storage.Series, ws annotations.Annotations, err error) {\n1046: \tfor it.Next() {\n1047: \t\tselect {\n1048: \t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n1049: \t\t\treturn nil, nil, ctx.Err()\n1050: \t\tdefault:\n1051: \t\t}\n1052: \t\tres = append(res, it.At())\n1053: \t}\n1054: \treturn res, it.Warnings(), it.Err()\n1055: }\n1056: \n1057: type errWithWarnings struct {\n1058: \terr      error\n1059: \twarnings annotations.Annotations\n1060: }\n1061: \n1062: func (e errWithWarnings) Error() string { return e.err.Error() }\n1063: \n1064: // An evaluator evaluates the given expressions over the given fixed\n1065: // timestamps. It is attached to an engine through which it connects to a\n1066: // querier and reports errors. On timeout or cancellation of its context it\n1067: // terminates.\n1068: type evaluator struct {\n1069: \tstartTimestamp int64 // Start time in milliseconds.\n1070: \tendTimestamp   int64 // End time in milliseconds.\n1071: \tinterval       int64 // Interval in milliseconds.\n1072: \n1073: \tmaxSamples               int\n1074: \tcurrentSamples           int\n1075: \tlogger                   *slog.Logger\n1076: \tlookbackDelta            time.Duration\n1077: \tsamplesStats             *stats.QuerySamples\n1078: \tnoStepSubqueryIntervalFn func(rangeMillis int64) int64\n1079: \tenableDelayedNameRemoval bool\n1080: \tenableTypeAndUnitLabels  bool\n1081: \tquerier                  storage.Querier\n1082: }\n1083: \n1084: // errorf causes a panic with the input formatted into an error.\n1085: func (ev *evaluator) errorf(format string, args ...any) {\n1086: \tev.error(fmt.Errorf(format, args...))\n1087: }\n1088: \n1089: // error causes a panic with the given error.\n1090: func (*evaluator) error(err error) {\n1091: \tpanic(err)\n1092: }\n1093: \n1094: // recover is the handler that turns panics into returns from the top level of evaluation.\n1095: func (ev *evaluator) recover(expr parser.Expr, ws *annotations.Annotations, errp *error) {\n1096: \te := recover()\n1097: \tif e == nil {\n1098: \t\treturn\n1099: \t}\n1100: \n1101: \tswitch err := e.(type) {\n1102: \tcase runtime.Error:\n1103: \t\t// Print the stack trace but do not inhibit the running application.\n1104: \t\tbuf := make([]byte, 64<<10)\n1105: \t\tbuf = buf[:runtime.Stack(buf, false)]\n1106: \n1107: \t\tev.logger.Error(\"runtime panic during query evaluation\", \"expr\", expr.String(), \"err\", e, \"stacktrace\", string(buf))\n1108: \t\t*errp = fmt.Errorf(\"unexpected error: %w\", err)\n1109: \tcase errWithWarnings:\n1110: \t\t*errp = err.err\n1111: \t\tws.Merge(err.warnings)\n1112: \tcase error:\n1113: \t\t*errp = err\n1114: \tdefault:\n1115: \t\t*errp = fmt.Errorf(\"%v\", err)\n1116: \t}\n1117: }\n1118: \n1119: func (ev *evaluator) Eval(ctx context.Context, expr parser.Expr) (v parser.Value, ws annotations.Annotations, err error) {\n1120: \tdefer ev.recover(expr, &ws, &err)\n1121: \n1122: \tv, ws = ev.eval(ctx, expr)\n1123: \tif ev.enableDelayedNameRemoval {\n1124: \t\tev.cleanupMetricLabels(v)\n1125: \t}\n1126: \treturn v, ws, nil\n1127: }\n1128: \n1129: // EvalSeriesHelper stores extra information about a series.\n1130: type EvalSeriesHelper struct {\n1131: \t// Used to map left-hand to right-hand in binary operations.\n1132: \tsignature string\n1133: }\n1134: \n1135: // EvalNodeHelper stores extra information and caches for evaluating a single node across steps.\n1136: type EvalNodeHelper struct {\n1137: \t// Evaluation timestamp.\n1138: \tTs int64\n1139: \t// Vector that can be used for output.\n1140: \tOut Vector\n1141: \n1142: \t// Caches.\n1143: \t// funcHistogramQuantile and funcHistogramFraction for classic histograms.\n1144: \tsignatureToMetricWithBuckets map[string]*metricWithBuckets\n1145: \tnativeHistogramSamples       []Sample\n1146: \n1147: \tlb           *labels.Builder\n1148: \tlblBuf       []byte\n1149: \tlblResultBuf []byte\n1150: \n1151: \t// For binary vector matching.\n1152: \trightSigs    map[string]Sample\n1153: \tmatchedSigs  map[string]map[uint64]struct{}\n1154: \tresultMetric the histogram caches by splitting the given vector into native and classic histograms.\n1169: func (enh *EvalNodeHelper) resetHistograms(inVec Vector, arg parser.Expr) annotations.Annotations {\n1170: \tvar annos annotations.Annotations\n1171: \n1172: \tif enh.signatureToMetricWithBuckets == nil {\n1173: \t\tenh.signatureToMetricWithBuckets = map[string]*metricWithBuckets{}\n1174: \t} else {\n1175: \t\tfor _, v := range enh.signatureToMetricWithBuckets {\n1176: \t\t\tv.buckets = v.buckets[:0]\n1177: \t\t}\n1178: \t}\n1179: \tenh.nativeHistogramSamples = enh.nativeHistogramSamples[:0]\n1180: Do not evaluate anything.\n1220: \t\t\tif enh.enableDelayedNameRemoval {\n1221: \t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewMixedClassicNativeHistogramsWarning(sample.Metric.Get(labels.MetricName), arg.PositionRange()))\n1222: \t\t\t} else {\n1223: \t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewMixedClassicNativeHistogramsWarning(\"\", arg.PositionRange()))\n1224: \t\t\t}\n1225: \t\t\tdelete(enh.signatureToMetricWithBuckets, string(enh.lblBuf))\n1226: \t\t\tenh.nativeHistogramSamples[idx].H = nil\n1227: \t\t\tcontinue\n1228: \t\t}\n1229: \t}\n1230: \treturn annos\n1231: }\n1232: \n1233: // rangeEval evaluates the given expressions, and then for each step calls\n1234: // the given funcCall with the values computed for each expression at that\n1235: // step. The return value is the combination into time series of all the\n1236: // function call results.\n1237: // The prepSeries function (if provided) can be used to prepare the helper\n1238: // for each series, then passed to each call funcCall.\n1239: func (ev *evaluator) rangeEval(ctx context.Context, prepSeries func(labels.Labels, *EvalSeriesHelper), funcCall func([]Vector, Matrix, *evaluator) rangeEvalAgg(ctx context.Context, aggExpr *parser.AggregateExpr, sortedGrouping []string, inputMatrix Matrix, params *fParams) (Matrix, annotations.Annotations) {\n1390: \t// Keep a copy of the original point slice so that it can be returned to the pool.\n1391: \torigMatrix := slices.Clone(inputMatrix)\n1392: \tdefer func() {\n1393: \t\tfor _, s := range origMatrix {\n1394: \t\t\treturn nil, annos\n1452: \t\t}\n1453: \t\tif params.HasAnyNaN() {\n1454: \t\t\tev.errorf(\"Ratio value is NaN\")\n1455: \t\t}\n1456: \t\tif params.Max() > 1.0 {\n1457: Assemble the output matrix. By the time we get here we know we don't have too many samples.\n1507: \tswitch aggExpr.Op {\n1508: \tcase parser.TOPK, parser.BOTTOMK, parser.LIMITK, parser.LIMIT_RATIO:\n1509: \t\tresult = 0\n1516: \t\tfor _, series := range result {\n1517: \t\t\tif len(series.Floats) > 0 || len(series.Histograms) > 0 {\n1518: \t\t\t\tresult[dst] = series\n1519: \t\t\t\tdst++\n1520: \t\t\t}\n1521: point spaced ev.interval apart, from series given offset.\n1528: // For every storage.Series iterator in series, the method iterates in ev.interval sized steps from ev.startTimestamp until and including ev.endTimestamp,\n1529: // collecting every corresponding sample (obtained via ev.vectorSelectorSingle) into a Series.\n1530: // All of the generated Series are collected into a Matrix, that gets returned.\n1531: func (ev *evaluator) evalSeries(ctx context.Context, series []storage.Series, offset time.Duration, recordOrigT bool) Matrix {\n1532: \tnumSteps := int((ev.endTimestamp-ev.startTimestamp)/ev.interval) + 1\n1533: \n1534: \tmat := make(Matrix, 0, len(series))\n1535: \tvar prevSS *Series\n1536: \tit := storage.NewMemoizedEmptyIterator(durationMilliseconds(ev.lookbackDelta))\n1537: \tvar chkIter chunkenc.Iterator\n1538: \tfor _, s := range series {\n1539: \t\tif err := contextDone(ctx, \"expression evaluation\"); err != nil {\n1540: \t\t\tev.error(err)\n1541: \t\t}\n1542: \n1543: \t\tchkIter = s.Iterator(chkIter)\n1544: \t\tit.Reset(chkIter)\n1545: \t\tss := Series{\n1546: \t\t\tMetric: s.Labels(),\n1547: \t\t}\n1548: \n1549: \t\tfor ts, step := ev.startTimestamp, -1; ts <= ev.endTimestamp; ts += ev.interval {\n1550: \t\t\tstep++\n1551: \t\t\torigT, f, h, ok := ev.vectorSelectorSingle(it, offset, ts)\n1552: \t\t\tif !ok {\n1553: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n1554: \t\t\t}\n1555: \n1556: \t\t\tif h == nil {\n1557: \t\t\t\tev.currentSamples++\n1558: \t\t\t\tev.samplesStats.IncrementSamplesAtStep(step, 1)\n1559: \t\t\t\tif ev.currentSamples > ev.maxSamples {\n1560: \t\t\t\t\tev.error(ErrTooManySamples(env))\n1561: \t\t\t\t}\n1562: \t\t\t\tif track the original sample timestamp.\n1567: \t\t\t\t\t// Info metric values should be 1 by convention, therefore we can re-use this\n1568: \t\t\t\t\t// space in the sample.\n1569: \t\t\t\t\tf = should be an info metric, with float samples: %s\", ss.Metric))\n1575: \t\t\t\t}\n1576: \n1577: \t\t\t\tpoint := HPoint{H: h, T: ts}\n1578: \t\t\t\thistSize := point.size()\n1579: \t\t\t\tev.currentSamples += histSize\n1580: \t\t\t\tev.samplesStats.IncrementSamplesAtStep(step, int64(histSize))\n1581: \t\t\t\tif ev.currentSamples > ev.maxSamples {\n1582: \t\t\t\t\tev.error(ErrTooManySamples(env))\n1583: \t\t\t\t}\n1584: \t\t\t\tif ss.Histograms == nil {\n1585: \t\t\t\t\tss.Histograms = reuseOrGetHPointSlices(prevSS, numSteps)\n1586: \t\t\t\t}\n1587: \t\t\t\tss.Histograms = append(ss.Histograms, point)\n1588: \t\t\t}\n1589: \t\t}\n1590: \n1591: \t\tif len(ss.Floats)+len(ss.Histograms) > 0 {\n1592: \t\t\tmat = append(mat, ss)\n1593: \t\t\tprevSS = &mat[len(mat)-1]\n1594: \t\t}\n1595: \t}\n1596: \tev.samplesStats.UpdatePeak(ev.currentSamples)\n1597: \treturn mat\n1598: }\n1599: \n1600: // evalSubquery evaluates given SubqueryExpr and returns an equivalent\n1601: // evaluated MatrixSelector in its place. Note that the Name and LabelMatchers are not set.\n1602: func (ev *evaluator) evalSubquery(ctx context.Context, subq *parser.SubqueryExpr) (*parser.MatrixSelector, int, annotations.Annotations) {\n1603: \tsamplesStats := ev.samplesStats\n1604: \t// Avoid double counting samples when running a subquery, those samples will be counted in later stage.\n1605: \tev.samplesStats = ev.samplesStats.NewChild()\n1606: \tval, ws := ev.eval(ctx, subq)\n1607: \t// But do incorporate the peak from the subquery.\n1608: \tsamplesStats.UpdatePeakFromSubquery(ev.samplesStats)\n1609: \tev.samplesStats = samplesStats\n1610: \tmat := val.(Matrix)\n1611: \tvs := &parser.VectorSelector{\n1612: \t\tOriginalOffset: subq.OriginalOffset,\n1613: \t\tOffset:         subq.Offset,\n1614: \t\tSeries:         make([]storage.Series, 0, len(mat)),\n1615: \t\tTimestamp:      subq.Timestamp,\n1616: \t}\n1617: \tif subq.Timestamp != nil {\n1618: \t\t// The offset of subquery is not modified in case of @ modifier.\n1619: \t\t// Hence we take care of that here for the result.\n1620: \t\tvs.Offset = subq.OriginalOffset + time.Duration(ev.startTimestamp-*subq.Timestamp)*time.Millisecond\n1621: \t}\n1622: \tms := &parser.MatrixSelector{\n1623: \t\tRange:          subq.Range,\n1624: \t\tVectorSelector: vs,\n1625: \t}\n1626: \tfor _, s := range mat {\n1627: \t\t// Set any \"NotCounterReset\" and \"CounterReset\" hints in native\n1628: \t\t// histograms to \"UnknownCounterReset\" because we might\n1629: \t\t// otherwise miss a counter reset happening in samples not\n1630: \t\t// returned by the subquery, or we might over-detect counter\n1631: \t\t// resets if the sample with a counter reset is returned\n1632: \t\t// multiple times by a high-res subquery. This intentionally\n1633: \t\t// does not attempt to be clever (like detecting if we are\n1634: \t\t// really missing underlying samples or returning underlying\n1635: \t\t// samples multiple times) because subqueries on counters are\n1636: \t\t// inherently problematic WRT counter reset handling, so we\n1637: \t\t// cannot really solve the problem for good. We only want to\n1638: \t\t// avoid problems that happen due to the explicitly set counter\n1639: \t\t// reset hints and go back to the behavior we already know from\n1640: \t\t// float samples.\n1641: \t\tfor i, hp := range s.Histograms {\n1642: \t\t\tswitch hp.H.CounterResetHint {\n1643: \t\t\tcase histogram.NotCounterReset, histogram.CounterReset:\n1644: \t\t\t\th := *hp.H // Shallow copy is sufficient, we only change CounterResetHint.\n1645: \t\t\t\th.CounterResetHint = histogram.UnknownCounterReset\n1646: \t\t\t\ts.Histograms[i].H = &h\n1647: \t\t\t}\n1648: \t\t}\n1649: \t\tvs.Series = append(vs.Series, NewStorageSeries(s))\n1650: \t}\n1651: \treturn ms, mat.TotalSamples(), ws\n1652: }\n1653: \n1654: // eval evaluates the given expression as the given AST expression node requires.\n1655: func (ev *evaluator) eval(ctx context.Context, expr parser.Expr) (parser.Value, annotations.Annotations) {\n1656: \t// This is the top-level evaluation method.\n1657: \t// Thus, we check for timeout/cancellation here.\n1658: \tif err := contextDone(ctx, \"expression evaluation\"); err != nil {\n1659: \t\tev.error(err)\n1660: \t}\n1661: \n1662: \tif ev.endTimestamp < ev.startTimestamp {\n1663: \t\treturn Matrix{}, nil\n1664: \t}\n1665: \n1666: \tnumSteps := int((ev.endTimestamp-ev.startTimestamp)/ev.interval) + 1\n1667: \n1668: \t// Create a new span to help investigate inner evaluation performances.\n1669: \tctx, span := otel.Tracer(\"\").Start(ctx, stats.InnerEvalTime.SpanOperation()+\" eval \"+reflect.TypeOf(expr).String())\n1670: \tdefer span.End()\n1671: \tif ss, ok := expr.(interface{ ShortString() string }); ok {\n1672: \t\tspan.SetAttributes(attribute.String(\"operation\", ss.ShortString()))\n1673: \t}\n1674: \n1675: \tswitch e := expr.(type) {\n1676: \tcase *parser.AggregateExpr:\n1677: \t\t// Grouping labels must be sorted (expected both by generateGroupingKey() and aggregation()).\n1678: \t\tsortedGrouping := e.Grouping\n1679: \t\tslices.Sort(sortedGrouping)\n1680: \n1681: \t\tif e.Op == parser.COUNT_VALUES {\n1682: \t\t\tvalueLabel := e.Param.(*parser.StringLiteral)\n1683: \t\t\tif !model.UTF8Validation.IsValidLabelName(valueLabel.Val) {\n1684: \t\t\t\tev.errorf(\"invalid label name %s\", valueLabel)\n1685: \t\t\t}\n1686: \t\t\tif !e.Without {\n1687: \t\t\t\tsortedGrouping = append(sortedGrouping, valueLabel.Val)\n1688: \t\t\t\tslices.Sort(sortedGrouping)\n1689: \t\t\t}\n1690: \t\t\treturn ev.rangeEval(ctx, nil, func(v []Vector, _ Matrix, _ [][]EvalSeriesHelper, enh *EvalNodeHelper) (Vector, annotations.Annotations) {\n1691: \t\t\t\treturn ev.aggregationCountValues(e, sortedGrouping, valueLabel.Val, v[0], enh)\n1692: \t\t\t}, e.Expr)\n1693: \t\t}\n1694: \n1695: \t\tvar warnings annotations.Annotations\n1696: \t\toriginalNumSamples := ev.currentSamples\n1697: \t\t// e.Param is the number k for topk/bottomk, or q for quantile.\n1698: \t\tfp, ws := newFParams(ctx, ev, e.Param)\n1699: \t\twarnings.Merge(ws)\n1700: \t\t// Now fetch the data to be aggregated.\n1701: \t\tval, ws := ev.eval(ctx, e.Expr)\n1702: \t\twarnings.Merge(ws)\n1703: \t\tinputMatrix := val.(Matrix)\n1704: \n1705: \t\tresult, ws := ev.rangeEvalAgg(ctx, e, sortedGrouping, inputMatrix, fp)\n1706: \t\twarnings.Merge(ws)\n1707: \t\tev.currentSamples = originalNumSamples + result.TotalSamples()\n1708: \t\tev.samplesStats.UpdatePeak(ev.currentSamples)\n1709: \n1710: \t\treturn result, warnings\n1711: \n1712: \tcase *parser.Call:\n1713: \t\tcall := FunctionCalls[e.Func.Name]\n1714: \t\tif e.Func.Name == \"timestamp\" {\n1715: \t\t\t// Matrix evaluation always returns the evaluation time,\n1716: \t\t\t// so this function needs special handling when given\n1717: \t\t\t// a vector selector.\n1718: \t\t\targ := unwrapStepInvariantExpr(e.Args[0])\n1719: \t\t\tvs, ok := arg.(*parser.VectorSelector)\n1720: \t\t\tif ok {\n1721: \t\t\t\treturn ev.rangeEvalTimestampFunctionOverVectorSelector(ctx, vs, call, e)\n1722: \t\t\t}\n1723: \t\t}\n1724: \n1725: \t\t// Check if the function has a matrix argument.\n1726: \t\tvar (\n1727: \t\t\tmatrixArgIndex int\n1728: \t\t\tmatrixArg      bool\n1729: \t\t\twarnings       annotations.Annotations\n1730: \t\t)\n1731: \t\tfor i := range e.Args {\n1732: \t\t\ta := e.Args[i]\n1733: \t\t\tif _, ok := a.(*parser.MatrixSelector); ok {\n1734: \t\t\t\tmatrixArgIndex = i\n1735: \t\t\t\tmatrixArg = true\n1736: \t\t\t\tbreak\n1737: \t\t\t}\n1738: \t\t\t// parser.SubqueryExpr can be used in place of parser.MatrixSelector.\n1739: \t\t\tif subq, ok := a.(*parser.SubqueryExpr); ok {\n1740: \t\t\t\tmatrixArgIndex = i\n1741: \t\t\t\tmatrixArg = true\n1742: \t\t\t\t// Replacing parser.SubqueryExpr with parser.MatrixSelector.\n1743: \t\t\t\tval, totalSamples, ws := ev.evalSubquery(ctx, subq)\n1744: \t\t\t\te.Args[i] = val\n1745: \t\t\t\twarnings.Merge(ws)\n1746: \t\t\t\tdefer func() {\n1747: \t\t\t\t\t// subquery result takes space in the memory. Get rid of that at the end.\n1748: \t\t\t\t\tval.VectorSelector.(*parser.VectorSelector).Series = nil\n1749: \t\t\t\t\tev.currentSamples -= totalSamples\n1750: \t\t\t\t}()\n1751: \t\t\t\tbreak\n1752: \t\t\t}\n1753: \t\t}\n1754: \n1755: \t\t// Special handling for functions that work on series not samples.\n1756: \t\tswitch e.Func.Name {\n1757: \t\tcase \"label_replace\":\n1758: \t\t\treturn ev.evalLabelReplace(ctx, e.Args)\n1759: \t\tcase \"label_join\":\n1760: \t\t\treturn ev.evalLabelJoin(ctx, e.Args)\n1761: \t\tcase \"info\":\n1762: \t\t\treturn ev.evalInfo(ctx, e.Args)\n1763: \t\t}\n1764: \n1765: \t\tif !matrixArg {\n1766: \t\t\t// Does not have a matrix argument.\n1767: \t\t\treturn ev.rangeEval(ctx, nil, func(v []Vector, _ Matrix, _ [][]EvalSeriesHelper, enh *EvalNodeHelper) (Vector, annotations.Annotations) {\n1768: \t\t\t\tvec, annos := call(v, nil, e.Args, enh)\n1769: \t\t\t\treturn vec, warnings.Merge(annos)\n1770: \t\t\t}, e.Args...)\n1771: \t\t}\n1772: \n1773: \t\t// Evaluate any non-matrix arguments.\n1774: \t\tevalVals := make([]Matrix, len(e.Args))\n1775: \t\tfor i, e := range e.Args {\n1776: \t\t\tif i != matrixArgIndex {\n1777: \t\t\t\tval, ws := ev.eval(ctx, e)\n1778: \t\t\t\tevalVals[i] = val.(Matrix)\n1779: \t\t\t\twarnings.Merge(ws)\n1780: \t\t\t}\n1781: \t\t}\n1782: \n1783: \t\targ := e.Args[matrixArgIndex]\n1784: \t\tsel := arg.(*parser.MatrixSelector)\n1785: \t\tselVS := sel.VectorSelector.(*parser.VectorSelector)\n1786: \n1787: \t\tws, err := checkAndExpandSeriesSet(ctx, sel)\n1788: \t\twarnings.Merge(ws)\n1789: \t\tif err != nil {\n1790: \t\t\tev.error(errWithWarnings{fmt.Errorf(\"expanding series: %w\", err), warnings})\n1791: \t\t}\n1792: \t\tmat := make(Matrix, 0, len(selVS.Series)) // Output matrix.\n1793: \t\toffset := durationMilliseconds(selVS.Offset)\n1794: \t\tselRange := durationMilliseconds(sel.Range)\n1795: \t\tstepRange := min(selRange, ev.interval)\n1796: \t\t// Reuse objects across steps to save memory allocations.\n1797: \t\tvar floats []FPoint\n1798: \t\tvar histograms []HPoint\n1799: \t\tvar prevSS *Series\n1800: \t\tinMatrix := make(Matrix, 1)\n1801: \t\tenh := &EvalNodeHelper{Out: make(Vector, 0, 1), enableDelayedNameRemoval: ev.enableDelayedNameRemoval}\n1802: \t\t// Process all the calls for one time series at a time.\n1803: \t\tit := storage.NewBuffer(selRange)\n1804: \t\tvar chkIter chunkenc.Iterator\n1805: \n1806: \t\t// The last_over_time function acts like offset; thus, it\n1807: \t\t// should keep the metric name.  For all the other range\n1808: \t\t// vector functions, the only change needed is to drop the\n1809: \t\t// metric name in the output.\n1810: \t\tdropName := e.Func.Name != \"last_over_time\"\n1811: \t\tvectorVals := make([]Vector, len(e.Args)-1)\n1812: \t\tfor i, s := range selVS.Series {\n1813: \t\t\tif err := contextDone(ctx, \"expression evaluation\"); err != nil {\n1814: \t\t\t\tev.error(err)\n1815: \t\t\t}\n1816: \t\t\tev.currentSamples -= len(floats) + totalHPointSize(histograms)\n1817: \t\t\tif floats != nil {\n1818: \t\t\t\tfloats = floats[:0]\n1819: \t\t\t}\n1820: \t\t\tif histograms != nil {\n1821: \t\t\t\thistograms = histograms[:0]\n1822: \t\t\t}\n1823: \t\t\tchkIter = s.Iterator(chkIter)\n1824: \t\t\tit.Reset(chkIter)\n1825: \t\t\tmetric := selVS.Series[i].Labels()\n1826: \t\t\tif !ev.enableDelayedNameRemoval && dropName {\n1827: \t\t\t\tmetric = metric.DropReserved(schema.IsMetadataLabel)\n1828: \t\t\t}\n1829: \t\t\tss := Series{\n1830: \t\t\t\tMetric:   metric,\n1831: \t\t\t\tDropName: dropName,\n1832: \t\t\t}\n1833: \t\t\tinMatrix[0].Metric = selVS.Series[i].Labels()\n1834: \t\t\tfor ts, step := ev.startTimestamp, -1; ts <= ev.endTimestamp; ts += ev.interval {\n1835: \t\t\t\tstep++\n1836: \t\t\t\t// Set the non-matrix arguments.\n1837: \t\t\t\t// They are scalar, so it is safe to use the step number\n1838: \t\t\t\t// when looking up the argument, as there will be no gaps.\n1839: \t\t\t\tcounter := 0\n1840: \t\t\t\tfor j := range e.Args {\n1841: \t\t\t\t\tif j != matrixArgIndex {\n1842: \t\t\t\t\t\tvectorVals[counter] = Vector{Sample{F: evalVals[j][0].Floats[step].F}}\n1843: \t\t\t\t\t\tcounter++\n1844: \t\t\t\t\t}\n1845: \t\t\t\t}\n1846: \t\t\t\t// Evaluate the matrix selector for this series\n1847: \t\t\t\t// for this step, but only if this is the 1st\n1848: \t\t\t\t// iteration or no @ modifier has been used.\n1849: \t\t\t\tif ts == ev.startTimestamp || selVS.Timestamp == nil \t\t\t\tev.samplesStats.IncrementSamplesAtStep(step, int64(len(floats)+totalHPointSize(histograms)))\n1865: \n1866: \t\t\t\tenh.Out = outVec[:0]\n1867: \t\t\t\tif len(outVec) > 0 {\n1868: \t\t\t\t\tif numSteps)\n1877: \t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1878: \t\t\t\t\t\tss.Histograms = append(ss.Histograms, HPoint{H: outVec[0].H, T: ts})\n1879: \t\t\t\t\t}\n1880: \t\t\t\t}\n1881: \t\t\t\t// Only buffer stepRange milliseconds from the second step on.\n1882: \t\t\t\tit.ReduceDelta(stepRange)\n1883: \t\t\t}\n1884: \t\t\thistSamples := totalHPointSize(ss.Histograms)\n1885: \n1886: \t\t\tif \t\t\tev.samplesStats.UpdatePeak(ev.currentSamples)\n1895: \n1896: \t\t\tif e.Func.Name == \"rate\" || e.Func.Name == \"increase\" {\n1897: \t\t\t\tmetricName := feature is enabled, check __type__ label\n1901: \t\t\t\t\t\ttypeLabel := inMatrix[0].Metric.Get(\"__type__\")\n1902: \t\t\t\t\t\tif typeLabel != string(model.MetricTypeCounter) && typeLabel != e.Args[0].PositionRange()))\n1911: \t\t\t\t\t}\n1912: \t\t\t\t}\n1913: \t\t\t}\n1914: \t\t}\n1915: \t\tev.samplesStats.UpdatePeak(ev.currentSamples)\n1916: \n1917: \t\tev.currentSamples -= len(floats) + totalHPointSize(histograms)\n1918: \t\tputFPointSlice(floats)\n1919: \t\tputMatrixSelectorHPointSlice(histograms)\n1920: \n1921: \t\t// The absent_over_time function returns 0 or 1 series. So far, the matrix\n1922: \t\t// contains multiple series. In most such cases:\n2404: \t\t//   (a) the overlap is significantly larger than the eval step; and/or\n2405: \t\t//   (b) the number of samples is relatively small.\n2406: \t\t// so a linear search will be as fast as a binary search.\n2407: \t\tvar drop int\n2408: \t\tfor drop = 0; floats[drop].T <= mint; drop++ {\n2409: \t\t}\n2410: \t\tev.currentSamples -= drop\n2411: \t\tcopy(floats, floats[drop:])\n2412: \t\tfloats = floats[:len(floats)-drop]\n2413: \t\t// Only append points with timestamps after the last timestamp we have.\n2414: \t\tmintFloats = floats[len(floats)-1].T\n2415: \t} else {\n2416: \t\tev.currentSamples -= len(floats)\n2417: \t\tif floats != nil {\n2418: \t\t\tfloats = floats[:0]\n2419: \t\t}\n2420: \t}\n2421: \n2422: \t// ...then the same for histograms. between two Vectors, excluding set operators.\n2631: func (ev *evaluator) VectorBinop(op parser.ItemType, lhs, rhs Vector, matching *parser.VectorMatching, returnBool bool, lhsh, rhsh the result Vector. Check whether those labels have already been added for\n2734: \t\t\t// the same matching labels.\n2735: \t\t\tinsertSig := metric.Hash()\n2736: \n2737: \t\t\tif !exists {\n2738: \t\t\t\tinsertedSigs = map[uint64]struct{}{}\n2739: \t\t\t\tmatchedSigs[sig] = insertedSigs\n2740: \t\t\t} else if _, duplicate := insertedSigs[insertSig]; duplicate {\n2741: \t\t\t\tev.errorf(\"multiple matches for labels: grouping labels must ensure unique matches\")\n2742: \t\t\t}\n2743: \t\t\tinsertedSigs[insertSig] = struct{}{}\n2744: \t\t}\n2745: \n2746: \t\tenh.Out = append(enh.Out, Sample{\n2747: \t\t\tMetric:   metric,\n2748: \t\t\tF:        floatValue,\n2749: \t\t\tH:        histogramValue,\n2750: \t\t\tDropName: returnBool,\n2751: \t\t})\n2752: \t}\n2753: \treturn enh.Out, lastErr\n2754: }\n2755: \n2756: func signatureFunc(on bool, b []byte, names ...string) func(labels.Labels) string {\n2757: \tif on {\n2758: \t\tslices.Sort(names)\n2759: \t\treturn func(lset labels.Labels) string {\n2760: \t\t\treturn string(lset.BytesWithLabels(b, names...))\n2761: \t\t}\n2762: \t}\n2763: \tnames = append([]string{labels.MetricName}, names...)\n2764: \tslices.Sort(names)\n2765: \treturn func(lset labels.Labels) string {\n2766: \t\treturn string(lset.BytesWithoutLabels(b, names...))\n2767: \t}\n2768: }\n2769: \n2770: // resultMetric returns the metric for the given sample(s) based on the Vector\n2771: // binary operation and the matching options.\n2772: func resultMetric(lhs, rhs labels.Labels, op parser.ItemType, matching *parser.VectorMatching, enh *EvalNodeHelper) labels.Labels {\n2773: \tif enh.resultMetric == nil {\n2774: \t\tenh.resultMetric = make(map[string]labels.Labels, len(enh.Out))\n2775: \t}\n2776: \n2777: \tenh.resetBuilder(lhs)\n2778: \tbuf := bytes.NewBuffer(enh.lblResultBuf[:0])\n2779: \tenh.lblBuf = lhs.Bytes(enh.lblBuf)\n2780: \tbuf.Write(enh.lblBuf)\n2781: \tenh.lblBuf = rhs.Bytes(enh.lblBuf)\n2782: \tbuf.Write(enh.lblBuf)\n2783: \tenh.lblResultBuf = buf.Bytes()\n2784: \n2785: \tif ret, ok := enh.resultMetric[string(enh.lblResultBuf)]; ok {\n2786: \t\treturn ret\n2787: \t}\n2788: \tstr := string(enh.lblResultBuf)\n2789: \n2790: \tif changesMetricSchema(op) {\n2791: \t\t// Setting empty Metadata causes the deletion of those if they exists.\n2792: \t\tschema.Metadata{}.SetToLabels(enh.lb)\n2793: \t}\n2794: \n2795: \tif matching.Card == parser.CardOneToOne {\n2796: \t\tif matching.On {\n2797: \t\t\tenh.lb.Keep(matching.MatchingLabels...)\n2798: \t\t} else {\n2799: \t\t\tenh.lb.Del(matching.MatchingLabels...)\n2800: \t\t}\n2801: \t}\n2802: \tfor _, ln := range matching.Include {\n2803: \t\t// Included labels from the `group_x` modifier are taken from the \"one\"-side.\n2804: \t\tif v := rhs.Get(ln); v != \"\" {\n2805: \t\t\tenh.lb.Set(ln, v)\n2806: \t\t} else {\n2807: \t\t\tenh.lb.Del(ln)\n2808: \t\t}\n2809: \t}\n2810: \n2811: \tret := enh.lb.Labels()\n2812: \tenh.resultMetric[str] = ret\n2813: \treturn ret\n2814: }\n2815: \n2816: // VectorscalarBinop evaluates a binary operation between a Vector and a Scalar.\n2817: func (ev *evaluator) VectorscalarBinop(op parser.ItemType, lhs Vector, rhs Scalar, swap, returnBool bool, enh *EvalNodeHelper, pos posrange.PositionRange) (Vector, error) {\n2818: \tvar lastErr error\n2819: \tfor _, lhsSample := range lhs {\n2820: \t\tlf, rf := lhsSample.F, rhs.V\n2821: \t\tvar rh *histogram.FloatHistogram\n2822: \t\tlh := lhsSample.H\n2823: \t\t// lhs always contains the Vector. If the original position was different\n2824: \t\t// swap for calculating the value.\n2825: \t\tif swap {\n2826: \t\t\tlf, rf = rf, lf\n2827: \t\t\tlh, rh = rh, lh\n2828: \t\t}\n2829: \t\tfloat, histogram, keep, err := vectorElemBinop(op, lf, rf, lh, rh, pos)\n2830: \t\tif err != nil && !errors.Is(err, annotations.PromQLWarning) {\n2831: \t\t\tlastErr = err\n2832: \t\t\tcontinue\n2833: \t\t}\n2834: \t\t// Catch cases where the scalar is the LHS in a scalar-vector comparison operation.\n2835: \t\t// We want to always keep the vector element value as the output value, even if it's on the RHS.\n2836: \t\tif op.IsComparisonOperator() && swap {\n2837: \t\t\tfloat = rf\n2838: \t\t\thistogram = rh\n2839: \t\t}\n2840: \t\tif returnBool {\n2841: \t\t\tif keep {\n2842: \t\t\t\tfloat = 1.0\n2843: \t\t\t} else {\n2844: \t\t\t\tfloat = 0.0\n2845: \t\t\t}\n2846: \t\t\tkeep = true\n2847: \t\t}\n2848: \t\tif keep {\n2849: \t\t\tlhsSample.F = float\n2850: \t\t\tlhsSample.H = histogram\n2851: \t\t\tif changesMetricSchema(op) || returnBool {\n2852: \t\t\t\tif !ev.enableDelayedNameRemoval {\n2853: \t\t\t\t\tlhsSample.Metric = lhsSample.Metric.DropReserved(schema.IsMetadataLabel)\n2854: \t\t\t\t}\n2855: \t\t\t\tlhsSample.DropName = true\n2856: \t\t\t}\n2857: \t\t\tenh.Out = append(enh.Out, lhsSample)\n2858: \t\t}\n2859: \t}\n2860: \treturn enh.Out, lastErr\n2861: }\n2862: \n2863: // scalarBinop evaluates a binary operation between two Scalars.\n2864: func scalarBinop(op parser.ItemType, lhs, rhs float64) float64 {\n2865: \tswitch op {\n2866: \tcase parser.ADD:\n2867: \t\treturn lhs + rhs\n2868: \tcase parser.SUB:\n2869: \t\treturn lhs - rhs\n2870: \tcase parser.MUL:\n2871: \t\treturn lhs * rhs\n2872: \tcase parser.DIV:\n2873: \t\treturn lhs / rhs\n2874: \tcase parser.POW:\n2875: \t\treturn math.Pow(lhs, rhs)\n2876: \tcase parser.MOD:\n2877: \t\treturn math.Mod(lhs, rhs)\n2878: \tcase parser.EQLC:\n2879: \t\treturn btos(lhs == rhs)\n2880: \tcase parser.NEQ:\n2881: \t\treturn btos(lhs != rhs)\n2882: \tcase parser.GTR:\n2883: \t\treturn btos(lhs > rhs)\n2884: \tcase parser.LSS:\n2885: \t\treturn btos(lhs < rhs)\n2886: \tcase parser.GTE:\n2887: \t\treturn btos(lhs >= rhs)\n2888: \tcase parser.LTE:\n2889: \t\treturn btos(lhs <= rhs)\n2890: \tcase parser.ATAN2:\n2891: \t\treturn math.Atan2(lhs, rhs)\n2892: \t}\n2893: \tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\"operator %q not allowed for Scalar operations\", op))\n2894: }\n2895: \n2896: // vectorElemBinop evaluates a binary operation between two Vector elements.\n2897: func vectorElemBinop(op parser.ItemType, lhs, rhs float64, hlhs, hrhs *histogram.FloatHistogram, pos posrange.PositionRange) (float64, *histogram.FloatHistogram, bool, error) {\n2898: \topName := parser.ItemTypeStr[op]\n2899: \tswitch {\n2900: \tcase hlhs == nil && hrhs == nil:\n2901: \t\t{\n2902: \t\t\tswitch op {\n2903: \t\t\tcase parser.ADD:\n2904: \t\t\t\treturn lhs + rhs, nil, true, nil\n2905: \t\t\tcase parser.SUB:\n2906: \t\t\t\treturn lhs - rhs, nil, true, nil\n2907: \t\t\tcase parser.MUL:\n2908: \t\t\t\treturn lhs * rhs, nil, true, nil\n2909: \t\t\tcase parser.DIV:\n2910: \t\t\t\treturn lhs / rhs, nil, true, nil\n2911: \t\t\tcase parser.POW:\n2912: \t\t\t\treturn math.Pow(lhs, rhs), nil, true, nil\n2913: \t\t\tcase parser.MOD:\n2914: \t\t\t\treturn nil\n2929: \t\t\t}\n2930: \t\t}\n2931: \tcase hlhs == nil && hrhs != nil:\n2932: \t\t{\n2933: \t\t\tswitch op {\n2934: \t\t\tcase parser.MUL:\n2935: \t\t\t\treturn 0, hrhs.Copy().Mul(lhs).Compact(0), true, nil\n2936: \t\t\tcase parser.ADD, parser.SUB, parser.DIV, parser.POW, parser.MOD, parser.EQLC, parser.NEQ, parser.GTR, parser.LSS, parser.GTE, parser.LTE, parser.ATAN2:\n2937: \t\t\t\treturn 0, nil, false, annotations.NewIncompatibleTypesInBinOpInfo(\"float\", opName, \"histogram\", pos)\n2938: \t\t\t}\n2939: \t\t}\n2940: \tcase hlhs != nil && hrhs == nil:\n2941: \t\t{\n2942: \t\t\tswitch op
