nil\n2947: \t\t\tcase parser.ADD, parser.SUB, parser.POW, parser.MOD, parser.EQLC, parser.NEQ, parser.GTR, parser.LSS, parser.GTE, parser.LTE, parser.ATAN2:\n2948: \t\t\t\treturn 0, nil, false, annotations.NewIncompatibleTypesInBinOpInfo(\"histogram\", opName, \"float\", pos)\n2949: \t\t\t}\n2950: \t\t}\n2951: \tcase hlhs != nil && hrhs != nil:\n2952: \t\t{\n2953: \t\t\tswitch op {\n2954: \t\t\tcase parser.ADD:\n2955: \t\t\t\tres, counterResetCollision, err := hlhs.Copy().Add(hrhs)\n2956: \t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n2957: \t\t\t\t\treturn 0, nil, false, err\n2958: \t\t\t\t}\n2959: \t\t\t\tif counterResetCollision {\n2960: \t\t\t\t\terr = annotations.NewHistogramCounterResetCollisionWarning(pos, annotations.HistogramAdd)\n2961: \t\t\t\t}\n2962: \t\t\t\treturn 0, res.Compact(0), true, err\n2963: \t\t\tcase parser.SUB:\n2964: \t\t\t\tres, counterResetCollision, err := hlhs.Copy().Sub(hrhs)\n2965: \t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n2966: \t\t\t\t\treturn 0, nil, false, err\n2967: \t\t\t\t}\n2968: \t\t\t\tif counterResetCollision {\n2969: \t\t\t\t\terr = annotations.NewHistogramCounterResetCollisionWarning(pos, annotations.HistogramSub)\n2970: \t\t\t\t}\n2971: \t\t\t\treturn 0, res.Compact(0), true, err\n2972: \t\t\tcase parser.EQLC:\n2973: \t\t\t\t// This operation expects that both histograms are compacted.\n2974: \t\t\t\treturn 0, hlhs, hlhs.Equals(hrhs), nil\n2975: \t\t\tcase parser.NEQ:\n2976: \t\t\t\t// This operation expects that both histograms are compacted.\n2977: \t\t\t\treturn 0, hlhs, !hlhs.Equals(hrhs), nil\n2978: \t\t\tcase parser.MUL, parser.DIV, parser.POW, parser.MOD, parser.GTR, parser.LSS, parser.GTE, parser.LTE, parser.ATAN2:\n2979: \t\t\t\treturn 0, nil, false, annotations.NewIncompatibleTypesInBinOpInfo(\"histogram\", opName, \"histogram\", pos)\n2980: \t\t\t}\n2981: \t\t}\n2982: \t}\n2983: \tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\"operator %q not allowed for operations between Vectors\", op))\n2984: }\n2985: \n2986: type groupedAggregation struct {\n2987: \tfloatValue     float64\n2988: \thistogramValue *histogram.FloatHistogram\n2989: \tfloatMean      float64\n2990: \tfloatKahanC    float64 // \"Compensating value\" for Kahan summation.\n2991: \tgroupCount     float64\n2992: \theap           vectorByValueHeap\n2993: \n2994: \t// All bools together for better packing within the struct.\n2995: \tseen                   bool // Was this output groups seen in the input at this timestamp.\n2996: \thasFloat               bool // Has at least 1 float64 sample aggregated.\n2997: \thasHistogram           bool // Has at least 1 histogram sample aggregated.\n2998: \tincompatibleHistograms bool // If true, group has seen mixed exponential and custom buckets, or incompatible custom buckets.\n2999: \tgroupAggrComplete      bool // Used by LIMITK to short-cut series loop when we've reached K elem on every group.\n3000: \tincrementalMean        bool // True after reverting to incremental calculation of the mean value.\n3001: }\n3002: \n3003: // aggregation evaluates sum, avg, count, stdvar, stddev or quantile at one timestep on inputMatrix.\n3004: // These functions produce one output series for each group specified in the expression, with just the labels from `by(...)`.\n3005: // outputMatrix should be already populated with grouping labels; groups is one-to-one with outputMatrix.\n3006: // seriesToResult maps inputMatrix indexes to outputMatrix indexes.\n3007: func (ev *evaluator) aggregation(e *parser.AggregateExpr, q float64, inputMatrix, outputMatrix Matrix, seriesToResult []int, groups []groupedAggregation, enh *EvalNodeHelper) annotations.Annotations {\n3008: \top := e.Op\n3009: \tvar annos annotations.Annotations\n3010: \tfor i := range groups {\n3011: \t\tgroups[i].seen = false\n3012: \t}\n3013: \n3014: \tfor si := range inputMatrix {\n3015: \t\tf, h, ok := ev.nextValues(enh.Ts, &inputMatrix[si])\n3016: \t\tif !ok {\n3017: \t\t\tcontinue\n3018: \t\t}\n3019: \n3020: \t\tgroup := &groups[seriesToResult[si]]\n3021: \t\t// Initialize this group if it's the first time we've seen it.\n3022: \t\tif !group.seen {\n3023: \t\t\t*group = groupedAggregation{\n3024: \t\t\t\tseen:                   true,\n3025: \t\t\t\tfloatValue:             f,\n3026: \t\t\t\tfloatMean:              f,\n3027: \t\t\t\tincompatibleHistograms: false,\n3028: \t\t\t\tgroupCount:             1,\n3029: \t\t\t}\n3030: \t\t\tswitch op {\n3031: \t\t\tcase parser.AVG, parser.SUM:\n3032: \t\t\t\tif h == nil {\n3033: \t\t\t\t\tgroup.hasFloat = true\n3034: \t\t\t\t} else {\n3035: \t\t\t\t\tgroup.histogramValue = h.Copy()\n3036: \t\t\t\t\tgroup.hasHistogram = true\n3037: \t\t\t\t}\n3038: \t\t\tcase parser.STDVAR, parser.STDDEV:\n3039: \t\t\t\tswitch {\n3040: \t\t\t\tcase h != nil:\n3041: \t\t\t\t\t// Ignore histograms for STDVAR and STDDEV.\n3042: \t\t\t\t\tgroup.seen = false\n3043: \t\t\t\t\tif op == parser.STDVAR {\n3044: \t\t\t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\"stdvar\", e.Expr.PositionRange()))\n3045: \t\t\t\t\t} else {\n3046: \t\t\t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\"stddev\", e.Expr.PositionRange()))\n3047: \t\t\t\t\t}\n3048: \t\t\t\tcase math.IsNaN(f), math.IsInf(f, 0):\n3049: \t\t\t\t\tgroup.floatValue = math.NaN()\n3050: \t\t\t\tdefault:\n3051: \t\t\t\t\tgroup.floatValue = 0\n3052: \t\t\t\t}\n3053: \t\t\tcase parser.QUANTILE:\n3054: \t\t\t\tif h != nil {\n3055: \t\t\t\t\tgroup.seen = false\n3056: \t\t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\"quantile\", e.Expr.PositionRange()))\n3057: \t\t\t\t}\n3058: \t\t\t\tgroup.heap = make(vectorByValueHeap, 1)\n3059: \t\t\t\tgroup.heap[0] = Sample{F: f}\n3060: \t\t\tcase parser.GROUP:\n3061: \t\t\t\tgroup.floatValue = 1\n3062: \t\t\tcase parser.MIN, parser.MAX:\n3063: \t\t\t\tif h != nil {\n3064: \t\t\t\t\tgroup.seen = false\n3065: \t\t\t\t\tif op == parser.MIN {\n3066: \t\t\t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\"min\", e.Expr.PositionRange()))\n3067: \t\t\t\t\t} else {\n3068: \t\t\t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\"max\", e.Expr.PositionRange()))\n3069: \t\t\t\t\t}\n3070: \t\t\t\t}\n3071: \t\t\t}\n3072: \t\t\tcontinue\n3073: \t\t}\n3074: \n3075: \t\tif group.incompatibleHistograms {\n3076: \t\t\tcontinue\n3077: \t\t}\n3078: \n3079: \t\tswitch op {\n3080: \t\tcase parser.SUM:\n3081: \t\t\tif h != nil {\n3082: \t\t\t\tgroup.hasHistogram = true\n3083: \t\t\t\tif group.histogramValue != nil {\n3084: \t\t\t\t\t_, _, err := group.histogramValue.Add(h)\n3085: \t\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n3086: \t\t\t\t\t\thandleAggregationError(err, e, inputMatrix[si].Metric.Get(model.MetricNameLabel), &annos)\n3087: \t\t\t\t\t\tgroup.incompatibleHistograms = true\n3088: \t\t\t\t\t}\n3089: \t\t\t\t}\n3090: \t\t\t\t// Otherwise the aggregation contained floats\n3091: \t\t\t\t// previously and will be invalid anyway. No\n3092: \t\t\t\t// point in copying the histogram in that case.\n3093: \t\t\t} else {\n3094: \t\t\t\tgroup.hasFloat = true\n3095: \t\t\t\tgroup.floatValue, group.floatKahanC = kahanSumInc(f, group.floatValue, group.floatKahanC)\n3096: \t\t\t}\n3097: \n3098: \t\tcase parser.AVG:\n3099: \t\t\t// For the average calculation of histograms, we use\n3100: \t\t\t// incremental mean calculation without the help of\n3101: \t\t\t// Kahan summation (but this should change, see\n3102: \t\t\t// https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/issues/14105\n3103: \t\t\t// ). For floats, we improve the accuracy with the help\n3104: \t\t\t// of Kahan summation. For a while, we assumed that\n3105: \t\t\t// incremental mean calculation combined with Kahan\n3106: \t\t\t// summation (see\n3107: \t\t\t// https://stackoverflow.com/questions/61665473/is-it-beneficial-for-precision-to-calculate-the-incremental-mean-average\n3108: \t\t\t// for inspiration) is generally the preferred solution.\n3109: \t\t\t// However, it then turned out that direct mean\n3110: \t\t\t// calculation (still in combination with Kahan\n3111: \t\t\t// summation) is often more accurate. See discussion in\n3112: \t\t\t// https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/issues/16714\n3113: \t\t\t// . The problem with the direct mean calculation is\n3114: \t\t\t// that it can overflow float64 for inputs on which the\n3115: \t\t\t// incremental mean calculation works just fine. Our\n3116: \t\t\t// current approach is therefore to use direct mean\n3117: \t\t\t// calculation as long as we do not overflow (or\n3118: \t\t\t// underflow) the running sum. Once the latter would\n3119: \t\t\t// happen, we switch to incremental mean calculation.\n3120: \t\t\t// This seems to work reasonably well, but note that a\n3121: \t\t\t// deeper understanding would be needed to find out if\n3122: \t\t\t// maybe an earlier switch to incremental mean\n3123: \t\t\t// calculation would be better in terms of accuracy.\n3124: \t\t\t// Also, we could apply a number of additional means to\n3125: \t\t\t// improve the accuracy, like processing the values in a\n3126: \t\t\t// particular order. For now, we decided that the\n3127: \t\t\t// current implementation is accurate enough for\n3128: \t\t\t// practical purposes, in particular given that changing\n3129: \t\t\t// the order of summation would be hard, given how the\n3130: \t\t\t// PromQL engine implements aggregations.\n3131: \t\t\tgroup.groupCount++\n3132: \t\t\tif h != nil {\n3133: \t\t\t\tgroup.hasHistogram = true\n3134: \t\t\t\tif group.histogramValue != nil {\n3135: \t\t\t\t\tleft := h.Copy().Div(group.groupCount)\n3136: \t\t\t\t\tright := group.histogramValue.Copy().Div(group.groupCount)\n3137: \t\t\t\t\ttoAdd, _, err := left.Sub(right)\n3138: \t\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n3139: \t\t\t\t\t\thandleAggregationError(err, e, inputMatrix[si].Metric.Get(model.MetricNameLabel), &annos)\n3140: \t\t\t\t\t\tgroup.incompatibleHistograms = true\n3141: \t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n3142: \t\t\t\t\t}\n3143: \t\t\t\t\t_, _, err = group.histogramValue.Add(toAdd)\n3144: \t\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n3145: \t\t\t\t\t\thandleAggregationError(err, e, inputMatrix[si].Metric.Get(model.MetricNameLabel), &annos)\n3146: \t\t\t\t\t\tgroup.incompatibleHistograms = true\n3147: \t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n3148: \t\t\t\t\t}\n3149: \t\t\t\t}\n3150: \t\t\t\t// Otherwise the aggregation contained floats\n3151: \t\t\t\t// previously and will be invalid anyway. No\n3152: \t\t\t\t// point in copying the histogram in that case.\n3153: \t\t\t} else {\n3154: \t\t\t\tgroup.hasFloat = true\n3155: \t\t\t\tif !group.incrementalMean {\n3156: \t\t\t\t\tnewV, newC := kahanSumInc(f, group.floatValue, group.floatKahanC)\n3157: \t\t\t\t\tif !math.IsInf(newV, 0) {\n3158: \t\t\t\t\t\t// The sum doesn't overflow, so we propagate it to the\n3159: \t\t\t\t\t\t// group struct and continue with the regular\n3160: \t\t\t\t\t\t// calculation of the mean value.\n3161: \t\t\t\t\t\tgroup.floatValue, group.floatKahanC = newV, newC\n3162: \t\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n3163: \t\t\t\t\t}\n3164: \t\t\t\t\t// If we are here, we know that the sum _would_ overflow. So\n3165: \t\t\t\t\t// instead of continue to sum up, we revert to incremental\n3166: \t\t\t\t\t// calculation of the mean value from here on.\n3167: \t\t\t\t\tgroup.incrementalMean = true\n3168: \t\t\t\t\tgroup.floatMean = group.floatValue / (group.groupCount - 1)\n3169: \t\t\t\t\tgroup.floatKahanC /= group.groupCount - 1\n3170: \t\t\t\t}\n3171: \t\t\t\tq := (group.groupCount - 1) / group.groupCount\n3172: \t\t\t\tgroup.floatMean, group.floatKahanC = kahanSumInc(\n3173: \t\t\t\t\tf/group.groupCount,\n3174: \t\t\t\t\tq*group.floatMean,\n3175: \t\t\t\t\tq*group.floatKahanC,\n3176: \t\t\t\t)\n3177: \t\t\t}\n3178: \n3179: \t\tcase parser.GROUP:\n3180: \t\t\t// Do nothing. Required to avoid the panic in `default:` below.\n3181: \n3182: \t\tcase parser.MAX:\n3183: \t\t\tif h != nil {\n3184: \t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\"max\", e.Expr.PositionRange()))\n3185: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n3186: \t\t\t}\n3187: \t\t\tif group.floatValue < f || math.IsNaN(group.floatValue) {\n3188: \t\t\t\tgroup.floatValue = f\n3189: \t\t\t}\n3190: \n3191: \t\tcase parser.MIN:\n3192: \t\t\tif h != nil {\n3193: \t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\"min\", e.Expr.PositionRange()))\n3194: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n3195: \t\t\t}\n3196: \t\t\tif group.floatValue > f || math.IsNaN(group.floatValue) {\n3197: \t\t\t\tgroup.floatValue = f\n3198: \t\t\t}\n3199: \n3200: \t\tcase parser.COUNT:\n3201: \t\t\tgroup.groupCount++\n3202: \n3203: \t\tcase parser.STDVAR, parser.STDDEV:\n3204: \t\t\tif h == nil { // Ignore native histograms.\n3205: \t\t\t\tgroup.groupCount++\n3206: \t\t\t\tdelta := f - group.floatMean\n3207: \t\t\t\tgroup.floatMean += delta / group.groupCount\n3208: \t\t\t\tgroup.floatValue += delta * (f - group.floatMean)\n3209: \t\t\t} else {\n3210: \t\t\t\tif op == parser.STDVAR {\n3211: \t\t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\"stdvar\", e.Expr.PositionRange()))\n3212: \t\t\t\t} else {\n3213: \t\t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\"stddev\", e.Expr.PositionRange()))\n3214: \t\t\t\t}\n3215: \t\t\t}\n3216: \n3217: \t\tcase parser.QUANTILE:\n3218: \t\t\tif h != nil {\n3219: \t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\"quantile\", e.Expr.PositionRange()))\n3220: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n3221: \t\t\t}\n3222: \t\t\tgroup.heap = append(group.heap, Sample{F: f})\n3223: \n3224: \t\tdefault:\n3225: \t\t\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\"expected aggregation operator but got %q\", op))\n3226: \t\t}\n3227: \t}\n3228: \n3229: \t// Construct the output matrix from the aggregated groups.\n3230: \tnumSteps := int((ev.endTimestamp-ev.startTimestamp)/ev.interval) + 1\n3231: \n3232: \tfor ri, aggr := range groups {\n3233: \t\tif !aggr.seen {\n3234: \t\t\tcontinue\n3235: \t\t}\n3236: \t\tswitch op {\n3237: \t\tcase parser.AVG:\n3238: \t\t\tif aggr.hasFloat && aggr.hasHistogram {\n3239: \t\t\t\t// We cannot aggregate histogram sample with a float64 sample.\n3240: \t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewMixedFloatsHistogramsAggWarning(e.Expr.PositionRange()))\n3241: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n3242: \t\t\t}\n3243: \t\t\tswitch {\n3244: \t\t\tcase aggr.incompatibleHistograms:\n3245: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n3246: \t\t\tcase aggr.hasHistogram:\n3247: \t\t\t\taggr.histogramValue = aggr.histogramValue.Compact(0)\n3248: \t\t\tcase aggr.incrementalMean:\n3249: \t\t\t\taggr.floatValue = aggr.floatMean + aggr.floatKahanC\n3250: \t\t\tdefault:\n3251: \t\t\t\taggr.floatValue = aggr.floatValue/aggr.groupCount + aggr.floatKahanC/aggr.groupCount\n3252: \t\t\t}\n3253: \n3254: \t\tcase parser.COUNT:\n3255: \t\t\taggr.floatValue = aggr.groupCount\n3256: \n3257: \t\tcase parser.STDVAR:\n3258: \t\t\taggr.floatValue /= aggr.groupCount\n3259: \n3260: \t\tcase parser.STDDEV:\n3261: \t\t\taggr.floatValue = math.Sqrt(aggr.floatValue / aggr.groupCount)\n3262: \n3263: \t\tcase parser.QUANTILE:\n3264: \t\t\taggr.floatValue = quantile(q, aggr.heap)\n3265: \n3266: \t\tcase parser.SUM:\n3267: \t\t\tif aggr.hasFloat && aggr.hasHistogram {\n3268: \t\t\t\t// We cannot aggregate histogram sample with a float64 sample.\n3269: \t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewMixedFloatsHistogramsAggWarning(e.Expr.PositionRange()))\n3270: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n3271: \t\t\t}\n3272: \t\t\tswitch {\n3273: \t\t\tcase aggr.incompatibleHistograms:\n3274: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n3275: \t\t\tcase aggr.hasHistogram:\n3276: \t\t\t\taggr.histogramValue.Compact(0)\n3277: \t\t\tdefault:\n3278: \t\t\t\taggr.floatValue += aggr.floatKahanC\n3279: \t\t\t}\n3280: \t\tdefault:\n3281: \t\t\t// For other aggregations, we already have the right value.\n3282: \t\t}\n3283: \n3284: \t\tss := &outputMatrix[ri]\n3285: \t\taddToSeries(ss, enh.Ts, aggr.floatValue, aggr.histogramValue, numSteps)\n3286: \t\tss.DropName = inputMatrix[ri].DropName\n3287: \t}\n3288: \n3289: \treturn annos\n3290: }\n3291: \n3292: // aggregationK evaluates topk, bottomk, limitk, or limit_ratio at one timestep on inputMatrix.\n3293: // Output that has the same labels as the input, but just k of them per group.\n3294: // seriesToResult maps inputMatrix indexes to groups indexes.\n3295: // For an instant query, returns a Matrix in descending order for topk or ascending for bottomk, or without any order for limitk / limit_ratio.\n3296: // For a range query, aggregates output in the seriess map.\n3297: func (ev *evaluator) aggregationK(e *parser.AggregateExpr, fParam float64, inputMatrix Matrix, seriesToResult []int, groups []groupedAggregation, enh *EvalNodeHelper, seriess map[uint64]Series) (Matrix, annotations.Annotations) {\n3298: \top := e.Op\n3299: \tvar s Sample\n3300: \tvar annos annotations.Annotations\n3301: \t// Used to short-cut the loop for LIMITK if we already collected k elements for every group\n3302: \tgroupsRemaining := len(groups)\n3303: \tfor i := range groups {\n3304: \t\tgroups[i].seen = false\n3305: \t}\n3306: \t// advanceRemainingSeries discards any values at the current timestamp `ts`\n3307: \t// for the remaining input series. In range queries, if these values are not\n3308: \t// consumed now, they will no longer be accessible in the next evaluation step.\n3309: \tadvanceRemainingSeries := func(ts int64, startIdx int) {\n3310: \t\tfor i := startIdx; i < len(inputMatrix); i++ {\n3311: \t\t\t_, _, _ = ev.nextValues(ts, &inputMatrix[i])\n3312: \t\t}\n3313: \t}\n3314: \n3315: seriesLoop:\n3316: \tfor si := range inputMatrix {\n3317: \t\tf, h, ok := ev.nextValues(enh.Ts, &inputMatrix[si])\n3318: \t\tif !ok {\n3319: \t\t\tcontinue\n3320: \t\t}\n3321: \t\ts = Sample{Metric: inputMatrix[si].Metric, F: f, H: h, DropName: inputMatrix[si].DropName}\n3322: \n3323: \t\tvar k int64\n3324: \t\tvar r float64\n3325: \t\tswitch op {\n3326: \t\tcase parser.TOPK, parser.BOTTOMK, parser.LIMITK:\n3327: \t\t\tk = min(int64(fParam), int64(len(inputMatrix)))\n3328: \t\t\tif k < 1 {\n3329: \t\t\t\tif enh.Ts != ev.endTimestamp {\n3330: \t\t\t\t\tadvanceRemainingSeries(enh.Ts, si+1)\n3331: \t\t\t\t}\n3332: \t\t\t\treturn nil, annos\n3333: \t\t\t}\n3334: \t\tcase parser.LIMIT_RATIO:\n3335: \t\t\tswitch {\n3336: \t\t\tcase fParam == 0:\n3337: \t\t\t\tif enh.Ts != ev.endTimestamp {\n3338: \t\t\t\t\tadvanceRemainingSeries(enh.Ts, si+1)\n3339: \t\t\t\t}\n3340: \t\t\t\treturn nil, annos\n3341: \t\t\tcase fParam < -1.0:\n3342: \t\t\t\tr = -1.0\n3343: \t\t\tcase fParam > 1.0:\n3344: \t\t\t\tr = 1.0\n3345: \t\t\tdefault:\n3346: \t\t\t\tr = fParam\n3347: \t\t\t}\n3348: \t\t}\n3349: \n3350: \t\tgroup := &groups[seriesToResult[si]]\n3351: \t\t// Initialize this group if it's the first time we've seen it.\n3352: \t\tif !group.seen {\n3353: \t\t\t// LIMIT_RATIO is a special case, as we may not add this very sample to the heap,\n3354: \t\t\t// while we also don't know the final size of it.\n3355: \t\t\tswitch op {\n3356: \t\t\tcase parser.LIMIT_RATIO:\n3357: \t\t\t\t*group = groupedAggregation{\n3358: \t\t\t\t\tseen: true,\n3359: \t\t\t\t\theap: make(vectorByValueHeap, 0),\n3360: \t\t\t\t}\n3361: \t\t\t\tif ratiosampler.AddRatioSample(r, &s) {\n3362: \t\t\t\t\theap.Push(&group.heap, &s)\n3363: \t\t\t\t}\n3364: \t\t\tcase parser.LIMITK:\n3365: \t\t\t\t*group = groupedAggregation{\n3366: \t\t\t\t\tseen: true,\n3367: \t\t\t\t\theap: make(vectorByValueHeap, 1, k),\n3368: \t\t\t\t}\n3369: \t\t\t\tgroup.heap[0] = s\n3370: \t\t\tcase parser.TOPK:\n3371: \t\t\t\t*group = groupedAggregation{\n3372: \t\t\t\t\tseen: true,\n3373: \t\t\t\t\theap: make(vectorByValueHeap, 0, k),\n3374: \t\t\t\t}\n3375: \t\t\t\tif s.H != nil {\n3376: \t\t\t\t\tgroup.seen = false\n3377: \t\t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\"topk\", e.PosRange))\n3378: \t\t\t\t} else {\n3379: \t\t\t\t\theap.Push(&group.heap, &s)\n3380: \t\t\t\t}\n3381: \t\t\tcase parser.BOTTOMK:\n3382: \t\t\t\t*group = groupedAggregation{\n3383: \t\t\t\t\tseen: true,\n3384: \t\t\t\t\theap: make(vectorByValueHeap, 0, k),\n3385: \t\t\t\t}\n3386: \t\t\t\tif s.H != nil {\n3387: \t\t\t\t\tgroup.seen = false\n3388: \t\t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\"bottomk\", e.PosRange))\n3389: \t\t\t\t} else {\n3390: \t\t\t\t\theap.Push(&group.heap, &s)\n3391: \t\t\t\t}\n3392: \t\t\t}\n3393: \t\t\tcontinue\n3394: \t\t}\n3395: \n3396: \t\tswitch op {\n3397: \t\tcase parser.TOPK:\n3398: \t\t\t// We build a heap of up to k elements, with the smallest element at heap[0].\n3399: \t\t\tswitch {\n3400: \t\t\tcase s.H != nil:\n3401: \t\t\t\t// Ignore histogram sample and add info annotation.\n3402: \t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\"topk\", e.PosRange))\n3403: \t\t\tcase int64(len(group.heap)) < k:\n3404: \t\t\t\theap.Push(&group.heap, &s)\n3405: \t\t\tcase group.heap[0].F < s.F || (math.IsNaN(group.heap[0].F) && !math.IsNaN(s.F)):\n3406: \t\t\t\t// This new element is bigger than the previous smallest element - overwrite that.\n3407: \t\t\t\tgroup.heap[0] = s\n3408: \t\t\t\tif k > 1 {\n3409: \t\t\t\t\theap.Fix(&group.heap, 0) // Maintain the heap invariant.\n3410: \t\t\t\t}\n3411: \t\t\t}\n3412: \n3413: \t\tcase parser.BOTTOMK:\n3414: \t\t\t// We build a heap of up to k elements, with the biggest element at heap[0].\n3415: \t\t\tswitch {\n3416: \t\t\tcase s.H != nil:\n3417: \t\t\t\t// Ignore histogram sample and add info annotation.\n3418: \t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\"bottomk\", e.PosRange))\n3419: \t\t\tcase int64(len(group.heap)) < k:\n3420: \t\t\t\theap.Push((*vectorByReverseValueHeap)(&group.heap), &s)\n3421: \t\t\tcase group.heap[0].F > s.F || (math.IsNaN(group.heap[0].F) && !math.IsNaN(s.F)):\n3422: \t\t\t\t// This new element is smaller than the previous biggest element - overwrite that.\n3423: \t\t\t\tgroup.heap[0] = s\n3424: \t\t\t\tif k > 1 {\n3425: \t\t\t\t\theap.Fix((*vectorByReverseValueHeap)(&group.heap), 0) // Maintain the heap invariant.\n3426: \t\t\t\t}\n3427: \t\t\t}\n3428: \n3429: \t\tcase parser.LIMITK:\n3430: \t\t\tif int64(len(group.heap)) < k {\n3431: \t\t\t\theap.Push(&group.heap, &s)\n3432: \t\t\t}\n3433: \t\t\t// LIMITK optimization: early break if we've added K elem to _every_ group,\n3434: \t\t\t// especially useful for large timeseries where the user is exploring labels via e.g.\n3435: \t\t\t// limitk(10, my_metric)\n3436: \t\t\tif !group.groupAggrComplete && int64(len(group.heap)) == k {\n3437: \t\t\t\tgroup.groupAggrComplete = true\n3438: \t\t\t\tgroupsRemaining--\n3439: \t\t\t\tif groupsRemaining == 0 {\n3440: \t\t\t\t\t// Process other values in the series before breaking the loop in case of range query.\n3441: \t\t\t\t\tif enh.Ts != ev.endTimestamp {\n3442: \t\t\t\t\t\tadvanceRemainingSeries(enh.Ts, si+1)\n3443: \t\t\t\t\t}\n3444: \t\t\t\t\tbreak seriesLoop\n3445: \t\t\t\t}\n3446: \t\t\t}\n3447: \n3448: \t\tcase parser.LIMIT_RATIO:\n3449: \t\t\tif ratiosampler.AddRatioSample(r, &s) {\n3450: \t\t\t\theap.Push(&group.heap, &s)\n3451: \t\t\t}\n3452: \n3453: \t\tdefault:\n3454: \t\t\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\"expected aggregation operator but got %q\", op))\n3455: \t\t}\n3456: \t}\n3457: \n3458: \t// Construct the result from the aggregated groups.\n3459: \tnumSteps := int((ev.endTimestamp-ev.startTimestamp)/ev.interval) + 1\n3460: \tvar mat Matrix\n3461: \tif ev.endTimestamp == ev.startTimestamp {\n3462: \t\tmat = make(Matrix, 0, len(groups))\n3463: \t}\n3464: \n3465: \tadd := func(lbls labels.Labels, f float64, h *histogram.FloatHistogram, dropName bool) {\n3466: \t\t// If this could be an instant query, add directly to the matrix so the result is in consistent order.\n3467: \t\tif ev.endTimestamp == ev.startTimestamp {\n3468: \t\t\tif h != nil {\n3469: \t\t\t\tmat = append(mat, Series{Metric: lbls, Histograms: []HPoint{{T: enh.Ts, H: h}}, DropName: dropName})\n3470: \t\t\t} else {\n3471: \t\t\t\tmat = append(mat, Series{Metric: lbls, Floats: []FPoint{{T: enh.Ts, F: f}}, DropName: dropName})\n3472: \t\t\t}\n3473: \t\t} else {\n3474: \t\t\t// Otherwise the results are added into seriess elements.\n3475: \t\t\thash := lbls.Hash()\n3476: \t\t\tss, ok := seriess[hash]\n3477: \t\t\tif !ok {\n3478: \t\t\t\tss = Series{Metric: lbls, DropName: dropName}\n3479: \t\t\t}\n3480: \t\t\taddToSeries(&ss, enh.Ts, f, h, numSteps)\n3481: \t\t\tseriess[hash] = ss\n3482: \t\t}\n3483: \t}\n3484: \tfor _, aggr := range groups {\n3485: \t\tif !aggr.seen {\n3486: \t\t\tcontinue\n3487: \t\t}\n3488: \t\tswitch op {\n3489: \t\tcase parser.TOPK:\n3490: \t\t\t// The heap keeps the lowest value on top, so reverse it.\n3491: \t\t\tif len(aggr.heap) > 1 {\n3492: \t\t\t\tsort.Sort(sort.Reverse(aggr.heap))\n3493: \t\t\t}\n3494: \t\t\tfor _, v := range aggr.heap {\n3495: \t\t\t\tadd(v.Metric, v.F, v.H, v.DropName)\n3496: \t\t\t}\n3497: \n3498: \t\tcase parser.BOTTOMK:\n3499: \t\t\t// The heap keeps the highest value on top, so reverse it.\n3500: \t\t\tif len(aggr.heap) > 1 {\n3501: \t\t\t\tsort.Sort(sort.Reverse((*vectorByReverseValueHeap)(&aggr.heap)))\n3502: \t\t\t}\n3503: \t\t\tfor _, v := range aggr.heap {\n3504: \t\t\t\tadd(v.Metric, v.F, v.H, v.DropName)\n3505: \t\t\t}\n3506: \n3507: \t\tcase parser.LIMITK, parser.LIMIT_RATIO:\n3508: \t\t\tfor _, v := range aggr.heap {\n3509: \t\t\t\tadd(v.Metric, v.F, v.H, v.DropName)\n3510: \t\t\t}\n3511: \t\t}\n3512: \t}\n3513: \n3514: \treturn mat, annos\n3515: }\n3516: \n3517: // aggregationCountValues evaluates count_values on vec.\n3518: // Outputs as many series per group as there are values in the input.\n3519: func (*evaluator) aggregationCountValues(e *parser.AggregateExpr, grouping []string, valueLabel string, vec Vector, enh *EvalNodeHelper) (Vector, annotations.Annotations) {\n3520: \ttype groupCount struct {\n3521: \t\tlabels labels.Labels\n3522: \t\tcount  int\n3523: \t}\n3524: \tresult := map[uint64]*groupCount{}\n3525: \n3526: \tvar buf []byte\n3527: \tfor _, s := range vec {\n3528: \t\tenh.resetBuilder(s.Metric)\n3529: \t\tif s.H == nil {\n3530: \t\t\tenh.lb.Set(valueLabel, strconv.FormatFloat(s.F, 'f', -1, 64))\n3531: \t\t} else {\n3532: \t\t\tenh.lb.Set(valueLabel, s.H.String())\n3533: \t\t}\n3534: \t\tmetric := enh.lb.Labels()\n3535: \n3536: \t\t// Considering the count_values()\n3537: \t\t// operator is less frequently used than other aggregations, we're fine functions and aggregations.\n3741: // Return isStepInvariant is true when the whole subexpression is step invariant.\n3742: // Return shoudlWrap is false for cases like MatrixSelector and than ratioLimit\n3936: \t//\n3937: \t// 0.0        ratioLimit                1.0\n3938: \t//  [---------|--------------------------]\n3939: \t//  [#########...........................]\n3940: \t//\n3941: \t// e.g.:\n3942: \t//   sampleOffset==0.3 && ratioLimit==0.4\n3943: \t//     0.3 < 0.4 ? --> add sample\n3944: \t//\n3945: \t// Else if ratioLimit < 0: add sample if rand() return the \"complement\" of ratioLimit>=0 case\n3946: \t// (loosely similar behavior to negative array index in other programming languages)\n3947: \t//\n3948: \t// 0.0       1+ratioLimit               1.0\n3949: \t//  [---------|--------------------------]\n3950: \t//  [.........###########################]\n3951: \t//\n3952: \t// e.g.:\n3953: \t//   sampleOffset==0.3 && ratioLimit==-0.6\n3954:
